text: With neighbors that love to blast their music, backfiring and loud cars and motorcycles, lawnmowers and construction noise outside my house, I can rather get mad and become that miserable old man, who calls the police every minute, that I can't watch sports in peace or I could find a solution. I am happily watching the NBA playoffs and I can barely hear anything, except the NBA game I am watching. Great bass and I can connect multiple devices, so if someone calls, I can take the call through the headphones and go back to the game. Now the only noise is music to my ears! Amazing sound, noise cancellation, and battery life for only $10? Not only that, but when the battery is low, it doesn't spam you with the "Low Battery" sounds that other earbuds do. If these ever go bad, I will be buying from this brand again! Nice sound with especially good base. Fairly comfortable fit but the tension seems a little tight for my wide head, but hopefully it will loosen a bit after repeated use. At this price I wasn't expecting to have a microphone and thus be able to take/reject phone calls while in use. It even announces who is calling! So this was an added surprise. It arrived fast and almost fully charged, so I was able to test it out right away.
product_name: Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Bluetooth Headphones,Bluetooth 6.0 Over Ear Wireless Headphones with 60H Playtime, Hi-Res Audio, Deep Bass, Memory Foam Ear Cups for Home Office Travel Black

text: I bought these wireless sports earbuds and they ve been a great choice, especially for exercising. The battery life is solid, just as advertised, which is perfect if you don t want to charge them all the time. As for sound quality, it s good for everyday use music, podcasts, and calls but don t expect premium-level audio. On the downside, the design might feel a bit bulky for some people, and the sound could have a bit more bass. Overall, they re a great value for the price, especially if you re looking for comfortable, durable earbuds for sports and daily use. Recommended for workouts and everyday listening. text: I got these on sale and figured for the price I would try them out for my kids. They were complaining that they couldn't hear the protective style over road noise and I tried those out and I also couldn't hear well. I warned them that these could get loud, but with the noise cancelling tech, they don't have to turn the volume up high. You immediately feel the noise cancelling when you turn them on. It almost feels like a pressure change and then the world goes quiet. My kids can now each listen to their tablets and don't complain about difficulty hearing when my friend and I chat in the front seat. It's a nice break for me and a nice break for them. These are extremely soft and comfortable. They're technically too big for my 4 yr old's head, but she works with them anyway because of how much better the sound is. My 6 yr old does great with them. They pair very easily. I got a set in pink and navy blue. The blue is nearly black, but again, the quality is so good my youngest doesn't care. I wanted to get them both pink, but I knew we'd have difficulty matching them to the correct tablets. This way we have pink with the pink tablet and blue with the purple tablet. It'd be cool if they made a nice purple color. The aux cord also works great and creates nice sound quality. I plugged them into my kids' electric piano and they were able to bang away to their hearts' delight without overstimulating me lol. The battery life also seems good. I legit forget to charge them and the girls are able to use them over several days. They don't use them more than an hour a day, but I know they aren't turning them off. So, the headphones are smart enough to shut down after enough time disconnected from a device to conserve battery power. The only negative I can think of for these is the head adjustment can pinch your finger really bad. I've punched my finger about three times trying to adjust it back to fully closed and each of my kids have pinched their fingers once. Now they ask me to deal with the adjustments. Other than that. I wish they made a sound protective kid size! The noise cancellation helps quiet outside noise, so having a max decibel system could be so nice for the kids without having difficulty hearing their devices. These Soundcore Q20i headphones work really well and do a great job blocking out noise.
product_name: Soundcore by Anker Q20i Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Headphones, Wireless Over-Ear Bluetooth, 40H Long ANC Playtime, Hi-Res Audio, Big Bass, Customize via an App, Transparency Mode (White)

text: Firstly, the color is soon cute, and exactly as pictured. I am so in love with the aesthetic of them. They also came fully charged, which was awesome so I got to use them immediately. Secondly, the quality is super good so far. Whether listening to podcasts, rap, or metal music, the sound quality has been perfect. They are compatible with both my switch 2 and my phone which is to be expected, but still happy there were no surprises or difficulties there. They aren't exactly noise canceling though. When they're on with no sound playing, other sounds are just dulled ever so slightly. When sound is playing, you can still hear people talking and other sounds like fans, but outside sounds aren't obtrusive and are blocked fairly well. I also really like how big the ear holes in the pads are, I wasn't expecting that. My whole ear fits inside them, which makes them super comfortable since they aren't smooshing the tops of my ears into the arms of my glasses. It also makes them really comfortable to wear while laying down, again because they don't smoosh my ears. I haven't had them long enough to really put them through the paces on charging or audio hours, but I will come back to add to this review a little later to comment on that. So far, so amazingly good! Super pleased with the quality for the price I paid. 5.0 out of 5 stars Incredible Value! Almost gives my Beats a run for their money! I can't gate keep any longer! text: These AirPods are my absolute favorite. I love these because unlike some Pro models, they don't come with foam or rubber earpieces which to me, are not very comfortable. These AirPods fit perfectly, fully compatible with all Apple devices, quality and durability is what one expects from Apple. Audio quality and features such as noise cancellation and adaptive audio are second to none. I also love the wireless charging case with speaker, so when I put it on a wireless charger, I get a chime telling me it's charging. Also in the latest released of all OS's, my devices tell me when the AirPods are fully charged. I'm very happy with these AirPods. My original AirPods were from pre-2020 I think? They were still working technically, but they weren t really holding a charge anymore and I had replaced the charging unit back in 2021, so it felt like instead of replacing that again maybe it was best to just get a new pair. I can t even express how IMPRESSED I was with the noise cancellation! It s honestly amazing coming from someone who has not had noise canceling headphones. The adaptive learning feature is also so cool - there s several features but I like the one that listen to hear if there s a conversation going and then it lowers the audio you re listening to. There s also a spacial awareness type one to hear what s going on around you, like you would without noise cancellation. But it s the noise cancellation that s the real star of the show. When I was on a plane and I put these in my ears without even listening to anything audio wise, it numbed the sound of the plane so much and made it so quiet it was amazing. They re small, and light in weight. The quality is just amazing - Apple does it again! They connected with my iPhone Pro 16 so well, instantly. I really can t say enough positive things they re worth every dollar. 16 Months In and Still My Everyday Go-To I use these AirPods for literally everything phone calls, music, podcasts, working around my Airbnb property, camping trips, running errands, you name it. I bought them in December 2024 and they've been in my ears almost daily since. Sound quality is still excellent. text: These are my first headphones I ve owned and I was looking for some all in one Goldilocks headphones and these are them! Aesthetics & Comfort: the color options are so pretty, they look good and actually fit on my very small head. They are soooo soft and I can wear them all day for work. Noise canceling: I don't do well with overlapping and competing noises, so I needed these to block out office chit chat at work, as well as my loud gaming hubby when I m gaming at home. I find that the noise canceling is good, but not 100% if you re listening to something quiet. You can customize it to different levels and save those modes of noise canceling, which I like if I need just a little bit of quiet or complete quiet. Sound quality: I think the sound quality is good, and furthermore, you can customize if you want more bass, mid, or treble. Connectivity & Mic: They connect to all my devices quite well and can be connected to 2 at once, so I can get calls on Teams from my work laptop while playing music from my phone. The mic seems great and these also do noise suppression so your callers don t hear background noise. Battery Life: I use these 8 hours each day and I charge them like twice a week :) I waited for them to be on sale for like $200 so these were an incredible investment and I m so happy with them. Comfy, high quality, and easy Goldilocks approved Comfortable, functional, and aesthetically pleasing to wear. The sound cancellation option is actually really good for the price. You have the option to turn it off as well. And when you download the app, you are able to toggle the noise cancellation strength along with connections and button command on which one controls what on your phone. (Ie: calls, Siri, volume, skipping). Sound is really good! The cushion on the ears and on the band on top is very nice. I don t have a melon head, but the size adjustment and flexibility is melon head friendly. Mind you, I m not headphone expert and upgraded from beat up cheap $70 JBL headphones that were begging me to stop using them. So, take the review how you will. Battery life last a looooong time. I just charge em for about 30 min to an hour when I m preoccupied and they practically last me the whole

text: Good price and great audio. The headphones are also pretty comfortable. I was looking for something to listen to my audibles with that would cancel excess noise and be comfortable even with my tragus and conch piercings. Those piercings, especially the conch can be irritated with pressure on them for long periods but the ear parts are soft on these headphones and they cover my ear so no pressure. I use these in the house, mostly in bed but I've used them while doing laundry and they're lightweight. AI recommended these as a budget option for the Sony/Bose headphones. These are my first headphones since the 90s! They are well made and at $50 I think they're an amazing find. The headset is adjustable and the buttons in the external ear cap allows me to change from noise canceling to aware, play/stop and increase and lower the volume without needing my phone. Had these for just over a year now and they have held up great. They are a very high quality with a durable build. Plus I got them in white and they don t show any signs of wear/tear. I did buy a case to store them in, so that probably plays a role in that. They fold up to be pretty small without the case but the case adds some bulk if you choose to get one. These don t feel cheap at all. The sound quality is excellent for the price as is the noise canceling. I use these almost every day at work for hours and hours on end and they never hurt. The battery life is also IMPRESSIVE. I feel like I don t charge them very much and they last forever between charges. I sit in a cubicle where you can hear everything around you, but if I have music or a podcast playing you can t really hear any outside noise. Without music playing you can still hear people speak, but I ve had a lot of ANC headphones (and some much more expensive ones) that can t block out voices without music playing. I have a pair of AirPods Max and a pair of AirPods Pro and these JLabs are by far the ones I wear the most. I haven t timed anything, but I would say they are the most comfortable for long periods of time and have the best battery life out of the three. I never have any issues connecting or staying connected to my phone. Really I have no complaints. If you want a good pair of ANC headphones these are amazing

text: I can honestly say that these headphones are worth the investment. There are times when I still need to plug in my headphones with a 3.5mm cable, so I like that I can use them wired or wirelessly. When it comes to using them wirelessly with Bluetooth, they are amazing. You can connect the headphones to a single dedicated device or to two devices simultaneously. For me, that is such a great feature. I use the headphones to connect to my computer for work purposes, but they're also connected to my phone as well. The sound quality is the best I've ever gotten from a set of headphones. I've never experienced noise-cancelling audio as precise as this. And for the times when you don't need to totally cancel everything out, the ambient sound mode provides audio that is just as good. The Sony Sound Connect app you use with these headphones is designed to deliver the best possible sound. The equalizer function lets you control how you experience whatever you're listening to, offering options like bright, relaxed, vocal, speech, and bass boost. I use these headphones all day while I work, and they hold a consistent charge. I still have plenty of battery life left at the end of the day, but I usually plug them up to charge when I'm done with them. The volume control buttons on the headphones are easy to use. The only thing that can be frustrating at times is that the button to stop/start a song or move to the next track is right in the middle of the volume buttons, so I end up hitting it when I want to raise the volume. However, it is nice that they've also included a button on the headphones to toggle between noise-cancellation and ambient sound. Again, I use these for work, so I wear them for the majority of the day. They fit well and are comfortable to wear. As the day wears on, my ears do get a little warm from the cushions around the headset, but compared to how much I like using the headphones, it's a minor issue. The only other item of note is that, given the investment, it would be nice to include a storage box or bag. If you're on the fence about buying these headphones, I highly recommend them. They are worth the investment, and you'll be pleased with both the sound and design. found a deal so decided to splurge a little out of my typical <$40 range. first thing was box felt empty, they
product_name: Sony WH-CH720N Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones Bluetooth Over The Ear Headset with Microphone and Alexa Built-in, Black New

I didn't custom set anything yet and sound was really good for my typical goth/techno/EDM. it also prompted to a 360 sound app- omg that was some crazy amazing quality but not venturing there at this time- but obviously these headphones have some quality to them sound wise. I usually have no issue with buying returns, and these were ok but definitely not mint, nor in a box, nor came with even the correct charger which was all that was included, and took 2 months to arrive (late) from the same coast I'm located- I'm least happy with the wait, seriously why?! the surprise was they were blue- I was settling for black because of the deal, and ended up with what I preferred! so if they last all-in-all twas probably worth the major discount and wait, if they don't then I'd probably still search them out again because it's taken a literal year and 24 pairs of exhausting headphone trials and returns to find something that actually fits and is lightweight and comfortable to boot~ WOOWOO! I kinda want to dock a star for the NC and not being honestly listed but for now I am definite 5 stars happy!

text: The Galaxy Buds 4 Pro feel like a well-rounded upgrade. Sound quality is the first thing that stands out. The sound is much more expansive and detailed, especially in the mid range where vocals usually get lost. The charging case is pocket friendly and the buds themselves have a refined finish that looks sharp without being flashy. It s clear that the goal was to create something that looks as good as it performs, and they ve definitely succeeded in making these feel like a high end piece of tech. The integration with my Galaxy S26 has been virtually seamless, which is a huge part of why I stick with the ecosystem. text: These are amazing. After going thru a lot of different pairs of other brands, I came back to these Apple ones that cannot be beat. They are a little more expensive than the other brands but there are reasons for it. The quality is great, the sound is incredible. They are truly noise cancelling. They are durable and the battery life is great. I have not tested the water resistance yet, but it may be handy in case you loose them in the washer. As much as I did not want to spend the money, they are really worth every penny. Charging is fast and great. Best decision ever to buy these. Your voice is isolated and nobody can hear ANYTHING going on in the background. So if you're on an important call and the dog barks, no big deal. If you are walking outside, wind, music, other voices - all filtered out. If you're starving and can't help but rudely eat a cracker on call - they can't hear you crunch! The other reason these airpods are awesome is because they FIT! The original AirPods pro did not fit me at all. I bought foam tips. Fail. I bought special over the ear hooks. Fail. So I just muddled my way through until they finally didn't work anymore and then I switched to Beats for a a few years. What changed it all? Talking with my bestie on the phone was driving me crazy. She was always on the move and making tons of noise that was ear piercing for me. When she unloaded the dishwasher it was a special kind of hell in my ears. She shopped for ear buds that would save my sanity, and she hit the jackpot with these. We spoke the other day and I was in shock - I couldn't hear her dog barking, her dishes clanking or....the final convincing moment: I could not hear her blowdryer! (I asked her to turn it on as a test). I had to buy them! I read reviews that said the fit was better so I figured I would give them a try. Last thing: This version has features I never knew I needed but LOVE. I was listening to an audiobook when I saw someone and started chatting and the book automatically stopped playing until the conversation was over. The Pro 3 also includes a hearing test and measures to protect your precious ears. Do I work for Apple? NO! I just wanted to share my enthusiasm to take the guesswork

text: I am very happy with my JBL TUNE headphones! Once charged they have incredible battery life and they are comfortable to wear more so than my ear buds. In my opinion. As far as the quality of sound you can get better but will spend more money doing so. I am satisfied with the audio quality and the different settings like noise canceling and ambient aware. Worth the money I spent on them. The app you download on your phone is disappointing. Not much there. I am happy with my purchase. Good balance of affordability and quality, easy connectivity, and good value for the money I spent. So far so good. I received the head phones in a timely manner. The box was smaller than I anticipated but housed the headset, the charging cable, a 1/8'" cable and the instructions. I turned on the Bluetooth on my computer, powered up the headset, hit the sync button and it connected automatically. The buttons are raised and easy to use. The volume buttons are pronounced and easy to operate. The headset did start to feel heavy after about an hour of use and the range of the Bluetooth is limited to about 15 foot. I may have had some interference with the microwave. The sound is very good with pronounced lows and clear highs. I didn't have to adjust the volume too much from noise discomfort. They did a good job at drowning out background noise. I would recommend them for the price. This is my first pair of noise canceling headphones. I really like these so far. Used them at work all day and barely used 20% of the battery. The sound quality is great. Haven't used them for calls yet so I can't say on the mic quality. They are pretty comfortable, but I do wish the ear cups were slightly larger. The quality is good and the controls on the headphones work great. It also has a companion app to download to customize the EQ and voice assist functions. Overall I would def recommend these as a great entry level noise canceling headphone. For the price they are a great buy for under 100 dollars. I will definitely recommend them to all my friends and family looking for a good pair of headphones on a budget.

text: Kinda expensive but well worth the price, you cannot hear anything in them, weither that be someone screaming at you or a blowdryer going off. The sound quality is also unreal, you can bop to this all day and it still hits the next time you put it on. Also the battery life is quite good, I barley have to charge it even after 3-4 days of heavy use. A lot better then my previous Hesh ANC headphones. Also while it isn't mentioned anywhere it is quite water resistant, I wash dogs for a living and they tend to shake and get you soaked and they have'nt rusted or degraded at all, at least from what I can see. Also unlike my previous headphones it has not pulled out my hair a single time when adjusting it, but that makes sense for $500 dollar headphones, the middle section bends quite a lot to but puts little force on your head so it remains quite comfortable for different sized heads. Also unlike my previous headphones again it never interupts you music to say low battery, it only does that once at 10% and lowers your music a tad to say that then ramps the volume back up again. I will say however I kinda like the physical buttons of my previous headphones a lot more then the swipe controls, even though they do not fail as often as you might think physical buttons just, never fail, its annoying when you go to up the volume and you skip the song thats all. It is a buy for me, and I would do it again, the ANC is just to good, you feel like your in a library all the time, even if someone is leafblowing right next to you, it is actually unreal. I purchased the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones from Amazon for $298.00 about two months ago as an upgrade to the WH-1000XM5 headphones, and I use them regularly. First and foremost, the Active Noise Cancelation (ANC) on these new headphones is the best in its class. With an array of 12 microphones, they capture and cancel-out most frequencies of background noise. It's a nice improvement over the older XM5 model. The sound quality is impressive. The frequencies are well-balanced with regard to bass, midrange and treble. They offer decent bass without being overbearing. They are lightweight and feel comfortable to wear, although they have small earcups which might not fit entirely over the ear
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: The frequencies are well-balanced with regard to bass, midrange and treble. They offer decent bass without being overbearing. They are lightweight and feel comfortable to wear, although they have small earcups which might not fit entirely over the ear. They also have a tight clamping force on the head, which probably loosens over time. This close fit could make the headphones feel uncomfortable if you wear eyeglasses, like I do. The battery life is decent, and they recharge quickly. These headphones offer a plethora of useful features like a transparency mode to hear external sounds and very clear phone calls. In summary, the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones are expensive and, depending on your individual taste, may or may not be worth the price. I gave them a borderline 5 star rating. My opinion is that if stellar ANC and great sound quality are your objectives, then these headphones might be right for you. If so, make sure that you purchase them while on sale because the regular price is way too expensive. I have a headphone problem, or so my wife says. I ve owned the Apple AirPods Max, the Sony XM5 s, the Bose QC and QC Ultra the QC 45 s and 35 s both I and II, the Sonos Ace, and the Beats Studio Pro. Going into my XM6 purchase, my favorite are/were the Bose QuietComfort Ultra. I bought the QuietComfort Ultra during Prime Day for an unbelievable $329! I have found that they offer the best combination of comfort, sound and ANC for the price. So, how does the XM6 compare? The ANC is second to none. I augment our central air with a window unit, which sits right behind my head, and I can completely eliminate its sound. But static sounds are pretty easily muffled by most of newer ANC headphones, so how about dynamic sounds like a tv or normal conversation? The XM6 excel there, too. Put simply, they offer the best ANC of any of the aforementioned headphones. They are also pretty comfortable and clamp down relatively tight, though I haven t found anything to be as comfortable as either the QC or QC Ultra. The XM6 are designed to be over ear headphones, yet my ear doesn t seem to fit inside the cup like other over ear headphones. This, in my opinion, makes them less-comfortable then they could/should be. However, the memory foam is a nice touch and does help.
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: , yet my ear doesn t seem to fit inside the cup like other over ear headphones. This, in my opinion, makes them less-comfortable then they could/should be. However, the memory foam is a nice touch and does help. The sound is well-balanced and pretty good right out of the box, though I had to make use of the ten-band EQ to get them to sound just a tad better than my QC. With my current EQ settings, I would say they offer the clarity of the Apple AirPods Max, but with the bottom end of my Bose QuietComfort. The bass isn t overpowering, it s actually pretty accurate (after EQ tinkering). In the final analysis, the XM6 offer the best sound of any of the headphones I mentioned, but not by much. Furthermore, the ANC is superior to everything else I ve owned, but not by a staggering amount. So, which should you buy? If you re like me and in search of the best possible sound, and won t settle for anything less than the best ANC, then get the XM6. If, however, you prioritize comfort and value, and don t mind making some very minor concessions regarding sound and ANC, then get the Bose QuietComfort or the QC Ultra (wait until you catch them on sale, which is pretty often). I hope that helps. Happy listening! Update: 6/02/2025: Intermittent crackling in left speaker. Had to return them. I have replaced them, though. Update 06/07/25: I received the replacement pair and everything seems great. I neglected to mention in my previous review how great the Sony app is and how customizable the XM6 are. First, you get a ten band EQ, instead of the Bose three bands. Additionally, there are some awesome (optional) features that are missing from Bose. Sony has speak to chat, which mutes your music and puts your headphones in aware mode when you speak. Also, there is an option to accept or reject calls by simply nodding or shaking your head, which is very cool. If you re into features like that and general customization, then the Sony XM6 are for you. UPDATE 06/17/2025. Started hearing crackling again in the right speaker this time. This time I did a Google search to see if others were having the same problem, and it seems like turning on DSEE Extreme could be the
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: UPDATE 06/17/2025. Started hearing crackling again in the right speaker this time. This time I did a Google search to see if others were having the same problem, and it seems like turning on DSEE Extreme could be the culprit at high volumes. The DSEE Extreme setting is supposed to upscale compressed files. I turned off the setting and no more crackling. I replayed the same part of the same song with and without it engaged, it it s definitely the DSEE Extreme. Consequently, I ve turned off that setting and now everything seems fine.
product_name: Sony WH-1000XM6 The Best Noise Canceling Wireless Headphones, HD NC Processor QN3, 12 Microphones, Adaptive NC Optimizer, Mastered by Engineers, Studio-Quality, 30-Hour Battery, Black

text: Made me say wow. jbl customer for life, because as you see in reviews they do their job. I still have two old ones beat up but still work. Long battery life, perfect connection, durable. Same sound quality and everything else from day one till the end which I am not even sure what will break them, they withstand lots of abuse. I have a cheap pair 30$ that were pretty good, just a cheap plastic design that broke when they fell. They still were usable though. The sound quality was cheaper but still ok, I didnt think to get new headphones right away. These were over the head style but on your ear pressed. I also had a more expensive $300 one that was cup style more comfy but the sound wasnt much better. Its the functionality that sold me over time. I cant imagine dealing with simple issues like connection or breaking with just careful use, poor sound, buzzing and list goes on. JBL doesnt ever have that. I did some research before getting these to find I love sub bass that shakes your ears which they delivered. It made me say wow. Ok so I tried listening to all music like rock, electric dance music and pop. Its more clear and better quality still but interestingly, if it doesnt have sub bass it doesnt bring out the best of these headphones 770nc. I got it just for the sub bass though so if you are into that, its worth the hype. Its in a lot of genres of rap. It performs beyond expectation with the functionaity and durability of jbl. It really does make me say wow. I doubt that skull candy or even dr dre beats sound that good, you should really try these out. Headphones and sound are a very subjective topic. So far for me, these 770nc live have been a good experience. Great bass, good clarity in mids, and uppers. Build quility is good. Metal slides and hinges. Nice touch, compared to a lot of plastic on other more premium brands. Comfortable ear and head padding. Seems like longevity won't be a problem with these. During workouts they stay in place, except for when laying on my back. Then they tend to slide off somewhat. I can move them so the headband is more on my farhead so they don't slide off. These phones sound really good with plenty of volume to spare. You don't have to crank them to the limit to bring all the sound out

I can mow the lawn without ANC on and the sound is deadened nicely. ANC adds even more deadening to the experience. The talk through is a nice feature. The track thats playing lowers, letting the outside speach come through. You can also hear your own voice nicely in this mode to prevent shouting. If you need the track to stop playing in this mode, just tap pause. They do automatically pause when moved away from your ear also. Caught a good sale on these, $119. Definitely worth that price. I have a pair of Skullcandy Crusher ANC's coming in two days, which were also on sale at $149. Will do a comparison to determine my favorite. Keep an eye on prices. They fluctuate often, you might catch a good sale. IMO, you can't go wrong with these JBL's, especially at the sale price. These are amazing headphones. I had tried out competitor headphones and was thoroughly not impressed with their low performance sound. I had read multiple reviews on these talking about how they had muddy sounding bass tones, and I do not find that factual at all. As a matter of fact, these things have such a smart application that comes with them. It will test your hearing and then set up the headphones to give you the best sound your ears can hear and up all the different frequencies so that whatever you re hearing range is you hear the sounds clearly and it doesn t drown them out. They still hit with solid bass and I can clearly hear every guitar note and the snare drum as if I m playing my own instruments. The beautiful part is my wife can stand 5 feet in front of me and nag away and I can t hear a word she says even at low volume because of the active noise cancellation they do and they also have another setting that allows some ambient sound to be heard so if you do need to hear things around you you still can. text: Yes, I am still learning and trying to figure out, how to deal with the ultra sensitive touch control features, on these little earbuds. Also, they can fall out of your ears, without warning, if you aren't paying good attention to their "fit", which tends to loosen over an extended time of wearing them. However, those minor issue are more than offset, by the small size, and the nice audio quality of these little wireless earbuds. But what caused me to go ahead and give them 5 stars, is the amazing range they have. Recently, I went out into our garage to do a some cutting on my table saw out there. Then, when I came back into the house, I realized that I had left my phone way over on the far side of our house in my workroom. And that is a good 60 plus feet from where my saw is. And there were also several wells between where I was out in our garage. and where my phone was, way over in my workshop. Then I got to thinking about how during that entire 10 minutes or so, out in the garage, I didn't hear one bit of the usual static, nor any of the normal sound irregularities, that I used to always hear from that same distance, with every other set of wireless earbuds I have ever used here at the house. So, if you are looking for a good, inexpensive set of wireless earbuds, with very nice sounding speakers, and especially earbuds with exceptional rage, there little gems may be just what you are looking for. Looked for some cheap temporary earbuds to get my through my work week, expecteing something sub par but.. after almost 3 years of using them I'm amazed by how great they are. -sound quality is surprisly GOOD crisp lows and highs and bass -Battery life is fantastic, lasts I'd say 6 7 hours ;] -Bluetooth has good range, very easy to set up, absolutely no problems -the case shows battery life of the individual unit and each earpiece Something I should add, they are not durable, a simple drop out of out of my ear on to concrete broke my right ear buds BUT gorilla patched it right up. text: I purchased the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 after reading many reviews....starting with the 1- & 2-Star reviews. I don't feel the need to read good reviews if I can be content with the bad reviews. Many of the bad and/or negative reviews I read were either about: - Bass overpowering/ inadequate ability to adjust - Noise Cancelling didn't work well enough - Comfort - The 'matte' finish got blemished too easily So to quickly touch on the BEST points about the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2. - ***** BLUETOOTH Distance WELL OVER 175+ FEET AWAY. Tested this over a 10-minute period in open, unobstructed line-of-sight........and over 60+ feet within a house, many rooms away. - ***** SOUND QUALITY is Clear, Crisp, and seems to auto-adjust (not sure if it does or not) for the music I am listening to....and I listen to pretty much all of the major genres...i.e. Rock, Rap, Country, and even Electronic Dance Music (EDM)....sounds great with all! - ***** COMFORT fit and holds to the head whether you are working out or working in the yard, the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 maintains comfortability! - ***** BATERY LIFE IS PHENOMENAL!!!! After my initial charge, I wore the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2 for a full day (8-hours of play time) and only used 20% of the battery....so the following day, I wore for about another full day again and used 20%....CRUSHER told me I had 60% battery remaining after 2-days of use. - ***** HANDS-FREE Voice control and SK app makes connecting to music easy once set-up properly! As for what most reviews "complained" about....I didn't see an issue with any of them really! - The intensity of the "Crusher" Bass 'can' be turned up/down, however it should be noted that the dial that rolls, also needs to be PUSHED in to make adjustments to bass output....but this is NOT like the "BASS KNOB" on your car radio....meaning, you can not adjust the bass the near-0...or even to a "4-level", to be honest. The headphones are made to put a bit more bass-bump into your ears. PERF
product_name: Skullcandy Crusher ANC 2 Wireless Over-Ear Bluetooth Headphones with Multi-Sensory Bass, Active Noise Cancelling, Up to 60 Hours Battery, Microphone for iPhone Android - True Black

text: NOB" on your car radio....meaning, you can not adjust the bass the near-0...or even to a "4-level", to be honest. The headphones are made to put a bit more bass-bump into your ears. PERFECT for gym time and those audiophiles! - I mentioned my thoughts about comfort above. - The MATTE FINISH IS THE BEST!!!!....(for me!) I have a pretty active lifestyle that includes wearing around the house....doing yard work....driving tractors and backhoes...gardening...etc. In the short time (2-1/2 weeks) I have had the SkullCandy Crusher ANC-2, I have dropped (or otherwise) them numerous times. The matte finish has never scratched, as I fear a "glossy finish" would have, making imperfections more obvious. I just take a disinfectant wipe to clean the headphones. - I feel MANY PEOPLE who "complained" about the ANC "noise cancelling" feature not working are CONFUSED as to what this is exactly! This DOES NOT mean that these headphones completely "soundproof" your ears!!! AND THIS ESPECIALLY doesn't work if you have no sound coming through the headphones at all!!! If you want those headphones, you're better off looking in an aviation-pilot store somewhere. BUT the ANC feature does do it's job in cancelling outside noise while listeninng to tunes at an adequate level. ---- BTW...I'm not one to ever write reviews for products, but I have been legitimately blown away by the SkullCandy Crusher ANC 2, that I felt a need to counter some of the negative reviews that made me ponder my decision to buy. It wasn't until a good friend of mine told me she loves her SkullCandy products, that I took a chance and purchased. I'm glad I did! TJ This product is beyond anything imaginable. I always lived skull candy but the comfort, level of detail, quality and god damn the bass is just other earthy!

text: Overall, I am quite happy with this speaker. I like the small size, and it is very easy to use and sync with Bluetooth. The sound quality is very nice for the size and price. The volume is good with no distortion that I noticed. I use the speaker every morning to start my day with upbeat music. I would have given it 5 stars if not for two reasons. First the speakers are on the sides of the unit. That would be fine except that they are exposed. There is a cover on them that looks kind of like the material you might normally see on a speaker, although it is definitely sturdier than that. However, with the speakers on the outside of the unit, makes them easier to damage. I think an interior speaker would be better for that reason. Second, I do think that the time it can run when fully charged is overstated. I use it about 40 minutes every weekday morning, but I had to charge it in only two weeks, definitely far short of the 24 hours that is claimed in the description. I cannot speak on the claim that it is waterproof yet. I will update this review if I get the opportunity test it. Overall, this was a good purchase for me.

text: Small and portable. Holds a charge well. Works great outdoors, originally purchased for use while in the pool. The flashing LEDs are cute but if you are watching something they can be distracting, but you can turn them off so that's good. My one complaint I have is that there is no easy way to monitor its battery charge. You don't know the battery is low until it starts beeping at you. This speaker is great!! Would recommend to anybody. It is very clear, easy to hear. The Bluetooth connects automatically which is super nice. It automatically turns off after so much time which is super handy. text: It is great if you buy it for the right purpose. I have purchased two sets. For casual listening to music from my iPhone or other Bluetooth devices, it is great. For my TV in my bedroom, it is great. Can turn up the volume and adjust frequencies with no distortion. It is perfectly fine for those uses. If you are an audiophile and demand perfection in frequency responses or in a home entertainment system with demanding requirements, you may be disappointed. But for the PC, casual use, Bluetooth, and normal TV uses, it is perfect. Great sound at a great price. Easy to set up. Easy to switch modes. And they look good too. Highly recommend. Good stereo speakers are a great investment. I've enjoyed my B&Ws for two decades. But having "a stereo" is becoming more uncommon. Many people these days only hear music through their cell phone or TV speakers. Perhaps a small BT speaker or a sound bar. I wanted to get my mom something better than her tablet to listen to music on, and was considering a single BT speaker, but discovered these sub-$100 bookshelf speakers. To be honest, I was skeptical, but the offer of physical stereo separation and bass ports was too much to pass up. No matter how cheap, when moving air there's just no replacement for displacement. They arrived well packaged & undamaged. Removing the grills for inspection did reveal a small bulge on the front of the right speaker, which appears to be a manufacturing defect. As it's not visible with the cover on, that's not a significant flaw. The finish does seem reasonably tough, a textured vinyl wood grain in a neutral dark brown that should go with just about any decor. Setup was easy, and it paired quickly with mom's iPad. I'm impressed with the presence of an optical input. It's not HDMI, but it wouldn't be hard to use the included optical cable to connect to a TV. Unlike a sound bar or portable BT, these speakers could serve as the center of an entire entertainment system: TV/gaming through the optical (plus music streaming with a smart TV), turntable or CD through the RCA inputs, and BT for phone or tablet streaming. No receiver or switches required. At this price that connectivity & utility is quite a value. So how do they sound? Pretty good. I'm sure all the components are as inexpensive as possible, but we've been making ported two-way speakers for decades.
product_name: Saiyin Bluetooth Bookshelf Speakers, 40W X 2 Powered TV Speakers with 4 Inch Woofer, Turntable Speakers with Optical/AUX Input/Subwoofer Line Out for PC and TVs

text: or switches required. At this price that connectivity & utility is quite a value. So how do they sound? Pretty good. I'm sure all the components are as inexpensive as possible, but we've been making ported two-way speakers for decades. You don't really need diamond tweeters to produce acceptable sound. People have been rocking out with the old silk domes for a long time. The bass ports are well-tuned & the low-end is as good as you can expect from a speaker this size. The mids are disappointing, but one thing I haven't seen other reviewers mention is the digital amp has three different modes: "movie/music/news". Clearly preset EQ profiles. I figured "news" would probably boost the mid/human voice range. Selecting that did help the issue, and I would recommend using that setting even for music for a flatter response. Some additional EQ tweaking would probably be a good idea, but I doubt my mom will notice. She would love listening to Dolly Parton on an old AM truck radio, so a slightly tinny sound is probably appropriate. Another option - if size is not an issue - would be to go with the 5" version of this speaker for a few dollars more. Nothing this size will ever compare to free-standing tower speakers, but every little bit helps. Just having speakers of any kind is a big step up from in-device (TV/tablet) speakers, and having two real two-way speakers is inherently acoustically better than any single-box solution, especially at this price. The appearance is good - to most people a speaker is a speaker - and the connectivity options are truly excellent.
product_name: Saiyin Bluetooth Bookshelf Speakers, 40W X 2 Powered TV Speakers with 4 Inch Woofer, Turntable Speakers with Optical/AUX Input/Subwoofer Line Out for PC and TVs

text: There's only so much one can reasonably expect from small Bluetooth speakers streaming music from the suboptimal DACs found in most phones and tablets. That's why I've got quality wired music systems in my home and studio for serious music listening. I do however use different Bluetooth speakers for different situations. I mainly listen to vintage jazz and some Americana at normal volumes. I'm pretty old and don't frolic on the beach or hang by the pool with vivacious young people listening to pop music on JBL Boom Boxes at deafening volumes. I have a little UE Wonderboom 4 for listening to podcasts while working outside. It's lightweight, rugged, inexpensive and sounds fine. For quiet background music while visiting with friends on the back porch I'll grab my Soundcore Boom 2 or Klipsch Detroit, both with decent sound, even at lower volumes. And I keep a used compact JBL Flip 7 that stays directly connected to my laptop for the occasional youtube video. It stays charged from the laptop and I don't have to connect it by Bluetooth every time I turn on my laptop. But for travelling by suitcase to vacation Air B&B's or condo rentals, I always take a pair of Klipsch Nashville speakers. Their shape makes them easy to pack, and once you figure out how to link them (!), they play in true stereo. They offer excellent, balanced sound with a tight, unmuddied bass. They are loud enough for a large room and feature good transparency and impressive soundstage. A close second choice would have been a pair of the Marshall Emberton II's. They have the same brick shape as the Klipsch but are even smaller and lighter. They too have very good sound but I prefer the the more robust sound of the Klipsch. And the Embertons do not link in true stereo separation, since each speaker plays "stereo sound." They would be a good choice however for someone travelling who wants to save even more space and weight. I went with the Klipsch because I found the sound quality and price (under $100 each) worth carrying the extra weight. The sound of this thing is incredible. I think it might be overkill even lol I was originally looked at the Austin for portability, but I wanted more bass/clarity and this thing over delivered for me. I was debating between this or Bose Soundlink, and ended up going Klipsch, mainly because I have their PC speaker system
product_name: Klipsch Nashville Portable Bluetooth Speaker with a 2.25" full range drivers for 360 degree audio, IP67 dust and waterproof rating, 24 hours of playtime for a premium live concert experience

Obviously the soundstage is limited by its width, but it seriously packs a punch for all genres of music. Size-wise, it s literally built like a brick. The Nashville measures 7 x 3.2 x 3.1 (a brick comes in at 7.6 x 3.6 x 2.2 ), and it has a good solid heft to it, though it only weighs about half as much as a brick. I ve seen a lot of talk about battery life, but I get an easy 12 hours at 50% volume. Yay!: 1. Sound quality and volume, obviously! 2. Looks cool! Not quite as retro as the Marshall speakers, but kind of along those lines. Honestly I d like it if the Klipsch branding was a little more prominent even. 3. IP67 so it s good to go in the rain, in the shower, or next to the pool. Nay!: 1. No strap, and from what I can tell, no easy or even semi-easy way to add one. I *LOVE* this speaker, but the lack of a strap limits it a bit. Tips: 1. No tip from me, but if you find a decent strap for it, pls let me know! Bottom line: Price-wise the Nashville falls into the JBL Flip 7 range, but in sound quality

text: Absolutely worth it... text: Easy set up. Great sound. I have a Sony TV, I connected it using the optical cable. I connected my phone via Bluetooth and everything works great. I am impressed by the bass even though it doesn t have separate bass reflex speaker. Amaizin value for the price. This is my third and hopefully final soundbar in my hunt for something that just works. I did a bit of research that landed me on the Bose TV Speaker. This is for a living room with a 20' ceiling and a TV mounted above the fireplace. With young kids, the TV is usually on their shows during the day or mom and dad trying to watch something in the evening. We aren't looking to be fully immersed in booming action movies, dialogue is the focus. The last one I had would drown out dialogue with background music and I was always trying to adjust the levels. This is perfect, I don't have to fiddle with it and it does a great job of staying consistent and I never have an issue hearing dialogue. It connected easily to my Roku TV and even supports Roku TV integration, allowing me to adjust things through the TV interface. I like that it's relatively small while packing powerful audio. The only problem I had may have been due to my TV - when I connected to the Bose over Bluetooth it messed up the hdmi-arc connection and my TV was no longer powering the Bose on and off, I had to factory reset the TV to resolve this and I haven't tried the Bluetooth connection since. Bottom line is that I didn't want to spend more than $200 and I just wanted something that would just work - apparently that is a unicorn in today's age of soundbars - and the Bose TV Speaker is that unicorn Bought a giant name brand TV for the family room but the sound was flat. A couple hundred bucks and a few hours later amazon delivered this. Now there is actually some bass and you can actually understand people talking. Wish I had known a decade ago that something this simple and cheap makes such a huge difference in the tv watching experience. I did have a few issues with it crashing at first, but a little research revealed a setting on the TV that causes the issue and since changing that, it hasn't happened again. For myself I don't see any point in buying one of the significantly more expensive systems.
product_name: Bose TV Speaker - Soundbar for TV with Bluetooth and HDMI-ARC Connectivity, All-in-One Compact Soundbar, Includes Remote Control, Black

text: I love the sound. This little Bluetooth speaker cost me a little more than half a Benjamin and turns my smartphone into a respectable sound system. Plus, it s compact, portable, battery powered (for approximately six to eight hours at high volumes w/quick recharge using the newest standardized rechargeable/data connection that has a small, rounded-rectangular pill -shaped reversible plug), waterproof, and incredibly drop-resistant. When I drop it, instead of worrying I tell myself, "Relax, it's a Sony, it will be fine," and it always is. This is actually my second one. I dropped the first speaker many dozens of times over the past four years (easily 50+) and it continued to play flawlessly. What finally pushed me to replace it wasn t normal wear and tear. During a cross-town move from Brooklyn to Manhattan, I carelessly packed it in an open box. A sudden stop from the moving van bounced it out of the unsecured box, and a heavy table ended up resting on top of it. Amazingly, it wasn t crushed or dented. The only issue was a slight decline in sound quality at higher volumes the bass was no longer as clean. Considering what it survived, I was thrilled to find another one just like it for sale on Amazon. Because it s affordable and so durable, I kept the original as a backup and bought a new one. I really like this speaker. It s very small/compact but has a very nice, high quality, clear sound. The Bluetooth connection is SUPER easy to use; I just downloaded the app and it connected immediately - no repeated set up attempts and no fuss! And the Bluetooth connection doesn't drop - even when switching back and forth between multiple phones/devices which is somewhat rare with these kinds of speakers. It has a strap with a "buckle" so you can strap it on to almost anything and it's also waterproof which gives me peace of mind since I'm using it in the bathroom to listen to while showering etc. It plays a LONG time on a charge but you can also keep it plugged in all the time with the easy to use USB-C port. It's not the loudest speaker in the world but given it's very compact size, I really wouldn't expect it to be and I'd prefer crisp sound over booming volume. I would NOT recommend this you are trying to find a speaker for a large room or to hear throughout multiple rooms but for smaller areas

text: Firstly, I am a 43 yr old woman and do not even like Transformers. I bought these because they kinda looked cool and were on sale for $10. text: These SteelSeries Arctis GameBuds have been a fantastic addition to my gaming setup! The quick-switch 2.4GHz + Bluetooth 5.3 connectivity makes it super easy to jump between Xbox, PC, PS5, Switch, and mobile without any hassle, I just switch modes and continue playing. The pairing process was quick and seamless every time. The active noise cancelling is surprisingly effective for earbuds and really helps when I m trying to focus in noisy environments. The sound quality is clear and balanced with good depth for both games and music. I also appreciate the mobile app support because it lets me fine-tune EQ settings and customize controls to my liking. The battery life is excellent, I m getting close to the advertised 40 hours with the Qi wireless charging case, which is super convenient. The case itself feels sturdy and life on the go is much easier because I can top up the battery without cords if I need to. I also love that they re water resistant (IP55) perfect for workouts or rainy days, and they ve held up great so far. Comfort is also top-notch; they stay in well during longer gaming sessions and the fit feels secure without being tight. Overall, these are high-quality gaming earbuds that combine excellent connectivity, great sound, long battery life, and convenience. Highly recommend for anyone who games across multiple platforms or just wants a reliable all-around set of earbuds. I have had these for about 7 months, and in that time I have had two instances where the case will not charge. It's happening right now as I write this. The support website for Steele Series is almost impossible to navigate, it took me so long to open a ticket using their automated "AI" system because my issue wasnt listed and it refused to connect me to an Agent. Once it did I was able to open a ticket but the charging function started working again on its own, so I didn't follow up on it. That was a bad idea because here I am now with the same issue. text: Solid Gaming Earbuds With Some Trade-Offs I ve spent some time with the SteelSeries Arctis GameBuds, and they deliver on their promise of being a true gaming-first pair of earbuds. The big win here is the 2.4GHz dongle connection, which makes a huge difference compared to standard Bluetooth. Audio is lag-free, voices in Discord are clear, and games like Call of Duty or Helldivers 2 feel responsive without the usual wireless delay. QuickSwitch between 2.4GHz and Bluetooth is seamless, so swapping from console to mobile is easy. Sound Quality: For gaming, the directional audio is strong enough to pick out footsteps and environmental cues. For music, they re fine but not outstanding; details tend to blur together, especially compared to higher-end Bose or Sony buds. If you re a casual listener, they ll be more than good enough; audiophiles may want something else. Comfort & Fit: Lightweight and secure, but not the most comfortable for marathon sessions. After 2-3 hours, I find myself needing a break. They re bulkier than AirPods or Sony buds, so if you want a super-low profile, keep that in mind. Noise Cancelling & Transparency: ANC works, but it isn t anywhere near Bose or Apple levels. It s enough to dull background noise (like an AC unit), but don t expect silence. Transparency mode is functional but comes with a faint hiss, good enough for a quick conversation without removing the buds, but not natural-sounding. Battery & Features: Battery life is excellent, easily lasting through long sessions with the charging case pushing up to 40 hours. Wireless Qi charging is a nice premium touch. The SteelSeries GG app offers plenty of EQ presets for different games, which is a fun extra. Mic quality is decent at a desk, better than built-in laptop mics, but not on the level of a dedicated microphone. Build Quality: The earbuds themselves feel solid, but the case is plasticky and not very premium. Bottom Line: If you re mainly buying for gaming performance, these are an excellent pick: zero lag, easy setup, versatile across platforms, and strong battery life. Just don t expect them to fully replace high-end music earbuds or noise-cancelling headphones. Great gaming earbuds with unique features, but not perfect for audiophiles or ANC fans. I've used a lot of ear

True to advertisement, charging was indeed fast using a higher powered USB-C charger. It went from pulsating green to solid green in no time to which I interpret as being fully charged. Opening the lid starts the pairing process and putting them in your ear you can hear your paired device audio output. They fit perfectly in my ears out of the box and are quite comfortable with the included tips. Don't even have to swap tips, bonus! And I already raved about the bass that it can hit low. The treble is good, too, not shrilly, but very pleasant. The mids aren't forward but not too laid back either. So far with the music I've tried, it's at least a b+ tier sound to my ears, easily beat the Air Pod Pro and even possibly be on par with the Moon Drop Space Travel. More comparisons will be necessary and I'll update with any further discoveries. The build quality and material used also means it will be quite durable. I chose to pair it with my Android phone running current up to date version with Developer Mode enabled. It even displays its name properly (unlike many cheap devices that use weird names). Even with Developer mode enabled, however, LDAC CODEC option is still grayed out. Sad face. I thought I saw somewhere in the description that it had LDAC support. I'll have to check with one of the AI models and see if it can be un-grayed out. Maybe download the app will help. Got these not really expecting much, just wanted something wireless that worked and didn t cost a ton. Ended up being a lot better than I thought. Battery life is probably the best part. I barely ever charge these things. I ll use them for a few days straight and then realize I probably should charge them, not because they died but just because it s been a while. The case holds a good amount too, so I m not constantly hunting for a charger. Noise canceling actually works, just don t expect it to completely block out the world. It cuts down background noise a lot, like fans, road noise, stuff like that. You can still hear people talking around you, which honestly isn t the worst thing

Bass hits hard, especially for rap, which is mostly what I listen to. It s not super detailed or anything, but it doesn t sound cheap or blown out either. Just good, clean, and loud enough. Connection has been surprisingly good. I can leave my phone in one spot and walk pretty far away without it cutting out. Around the house, outside, whatever it stays connected. They ve also held up better than I expected. I ve worn them in the rain, no problems. Used them while walking around, riding in a boat, just being out and about, and they stay in my ears without constantly needing to fix them. First Impressions This initial review is mostly for sound only. I am referencing these to my Technics AZ Series, which to this day, by far, the best sounding, clearest, most enjoyable tws earbuds I have come across. This goes for soundstage, depth, clarity across ranges, and what I consider faithfulness in sound reproduction, what Dre refers to as how the music is meant to be heard. Only the Technics has been able to shine across all these dimensions, coming from a horn, drums, percussion, and guitar player. That said, against this reference the BP1s in Noise Cancelling mode holds its own against my Technics in sound clarity, particularly with the bass as oft mentioned, and the highs which are close, but noticeably not par. Fades, sizzles, done right. This is impressive for the price, a mere fraction of what the Technics is at. Also the battery life is much, much better. Much less range anxiety. LDAC needs to be vetted more, particularly with an LDAC supported source, one of my fav being the Quad DAC LG G series, if you can find one that works. text: I was an early adopter of the Anker brand when they first introduced their big budget brick speakers many years back. Since then, I have purchased several different models of earbuds and headphones. I keep going back because every item has been rock solid in performance and at a fraction of the price you'd pay for the prestige brands. As for the Space One, it was another excellent value buy. Noise-cancel, sound quality, comfort, and battery life are superb. I'd say these are a huge step up in comfort from my last pair with the mega cushion around the headband and ear cups. They also have an app to customize more specs but I haven't explored it yet. I would absolutely recommend these headphones and the Anker brand at large based on my own longterm experience with their products! These headphones are amazing! I ve used them for over a year now and they have served me well. The noise cancellation is fantastic for people who get anxious with a lot of noise. It stays charged for a long time and charges quickly. The bass with these and overall sound quality is excellent. These headphones connect to two devices as promised. They are very comfortable and durable. I ve used them on planes and while sleeping and they haven t broken. I have found that they are bulky when wearing so they look awkward. It would be better if you could clean the insides, mine are white and they ve discolored a lot.

text: Soundcore is the GOAT. The space one pro easily competes with and beats out many of its competitors in the $400-$500 for a fraction of the price. Both objectively and subjectively. Objectively; well made triple composite drivers offer high fidelity sound and nearly no distortion. For clarity and bass you can't do much better. This headset also includes the best version of Bluetooth with all the best audio codecs. The headset is very comfortable, lightweight and the most foldable. Battery life is absurd, the headset can last all day without charging and 5 minutes of charging gives you 8 hours. Noise cancelling while not top tier like Sony or bose is pretty darn good and will be more than satisfactory for most people. Full customization with the soundcore app so you can tune these exactly to your liking. Subjectively; just look up Wired Magazines blind headphone test by top sound engineers to pick their favorite sounding headset. The results shockingly put soundcore space one pro at #1 above the Sony flagship XM6, bose qc ultra 2, and Bowers and Wilkins flagship (like $500 more expensive than space one pro) These are so comfortable and work well. They aren t 100% noise cancelling (louder sounds like a lawn mower can still be heard), but for the price point they are perfect for me. I have ADHD and am sensitive to sounds, so I love that these are comfortable enough to wear for long periods of time while I work or shop. I got the white color and love the look. The material seems like it s good quality and the audio quality is great. The controls are intuitive as well.

text: After extensive research and watching YouTubers do sound tests, I decided on this headset, and I'm glad I did. The active noise canceling on the microphone is outstanding I've had music blasting and even vacuumed in the background, and no one could hear a thing. I bought this headset specifically for my work-from-home job, and being able to listen to music from my stereo while working has made my days much more enjoyable. So far, in Teams meetings, the mic has performed flawlessly, and I ll update my review once I ve used it for client calls. I also appreciate the dual functionality of Bluetooth and USB connectivity, which is a must for companies that require wired headsets. The automatic mute feature when the dongle is flipped up is incredibly convenient, and it works seamlessly on Teams. My only issue is that the headset tends to fall off easily when I look down. I m used to wearing a different brand that stays securely in place, so this has been a bit frustrating. I may try adding a headset headband cover to help hold it in place. Overall, it s a great purchase for noise cancellation and work productivity with just a minor adjustment needed for fit. As for the noise canceling on the speakers, I can tell it works because I notice a clear difference when I put them on versus when I take them off. That said, when I wore them to the gym, I could still hear the music blasting outside the headphones much more than with my high-end Sony ones. Also, while the music quality isn t as powerful as a high-end Sony headset, that s not what I use these for. The strength of this Jabra headset lies in ensuring the person on the other end of the call doesn t hear any background noise, which makes it perfect for my work-from-home needs. text: My audio background is "sweet spot" grade audio. Not cheap, not placebo tier, but Goldilocks zone. Think Superlux 668B, SHP9500, Galaxy Buds 3 Pro, Galaxy buds 2, an old Sennheiser BT 4.40 and so on. This falls exactly into that price zone and it absolutely crushes all of them on the thing I care about the most: clarity. TLDR if you need THE BEST noise canceling in this price bracket, get this. I cannot stand muddy or compressed sounding audio like the Sennheiser had. I like being able to hear every layer of audio clearly and this accomplishes that. Comfort wise you'll forget you're wearing these for an hour or so. These are the most comfortable closed backs that I have (not as good as the open backs+ velour pads I have, but it is what it is if you want good isolation). If there's one nitpick I have is that the clamping force is too weak for you to lay down with it unless you have a big head. It'll just slide right off your ears. I use these primarily to help me deal with my sound sensitivity post TBI. The sound of mowers in my neighborhood was so painful that I had to put on Loops and headphones just to try to block it. This will do it all on its own perfectly! Galaxy Buds of any variety I had couldn't stop it, but this sure can. Mic quality: this is totally sufficient for work and phone calls when indoors. Haven't tried outside yet but Sound Guys have a demo and it's not great for that. There's a 3.5mm jack on it so I can connect it to my desktop with my Vmoda Boom Pro for a better mic and a wired connection. Software is a little weird. I don't know why they bothered to make their own janky and poor AI assistant, but that's off by default: it'll use your phone's one instead. I thought I'd have to fiddle with EQ to get it to sound good, but the default tuning is actually my favorite through my testing. I got this on launch day because I happened to need a solution to the mower noise RIGHT NOW but I bet there will be some sweet sales frequently knowing the brand, but I thought it was worth it at full price, even as miserly as I am. It comes with a short type
product_name: Soundcore Space 2 by Anker, Active Noise Cancelling Headphones, Comfortable Fit, 50H Playtime, LDAC Hi-Res Wireless Audio, Dual Connection, Clear Calls, Bluetooth 6.1, Nap Mode, Wearing Detection

text: happened to need a solution to the mower noise RIGHT NOW but I bet there will be some sweet sales frequently knowing the brand, but I thought it was worth it at full price, even as miserly as I am. It comes with a short type C cable (you already have a bazillion, here's one more) and a short super thin 3.5mm cable. Maybe ok for connecting to your laptop. Multi point works great, which was my #1 pet peeve with the Galaxy Buds series is that they DIDN'T do that. It's basically seamless on here and I just don't think about switching. I now own both this pair and the space one, not the space one pro. For the price, this is an upgrade on every single feature you can think of over the space one. I tried both headphones side by side today, comparing them to the same track with the same EQ, and the winner is pretty clear to me. Space two has bigger soundstage, better imaging, noise cancellation is slightly better, however I was pretty impressed with how good the NC is on the original Space one which is really an ultra budget NC headphone right now. For only $120, plus a free case right now, you really can't do no wrong, this is probably the budget king of NC headphone on the market right now. And one thing it does above all else, is the comfort. One thing I hated about over the head headphones is that most of are very uncomfortable, this is not true for this fresh pair. It is indeed as advertised, extremely comfortable, although the materials feels no different to the touch on the Space one, the Space two just feels better when wearing for a longer period of time. It's also a bit heavier than the Space one, but I assume that's because the drivers are slightly better on the Space two. Why do I take a star off? Because despite the pros, this pair still sound like a budget pair of headphone, no surprise here since you get what you pay for. The NC sound is extremely modified to the point that Bass is still very forward and feels uncomfortable, even with hearID tuned EQ. It does not sound like a washed down premium sounded headphone, instead it does sound like a pair of very well dressed budget headphone. So if you are expecting Bose or Sony XM level of sound for only $120, look elsewhere. This is for people with a budget mindset that wants respectable NC performance and extreme comfort

I just received the new Soundcore Space 2 on launch day and I m already impressed. Here are a few things I ve noticed right out of the box: The Launch Bonus: Just a heads up for everyone I purchased these during the launch window and they came with a FREE hardshell travel case (the grey textured one in my photos). To be clear, the retail box normally includes a black soft carry pouch, so getting the hard case as an extra gift was a fantastic value. It feels very sturdy and fits the headphones perfectly. Weight & Comfort: These are incredibly lightweight (261g). I ve been wearing them while setting up and they feel like they re barely there. The memory foam is soft and doesn t feel like it s clamping too hard on my head. Setup & Firmware: As soon as I paired them with my iPad and opened the Soundcore app, a firmware update popped up. It took about 5 10 minutes to install, but it really seemed to stabilize the connection. Noise Cancellation (ANC 3.0): The Adaptive ANC is very solid. It easily handles the low hum of my apartment. For sharper sounds like the TV, I found that switching to "Manual Mode - Level 5" in the app works best to really seal out the world. Battery for Days: The box confirms 70 hours of battery (50 with ANC), which is class-leading for this price. Pro-Tip: If you re using these for gaming or movies, make sure to enable the "Dual Device Connection" in the settings. I have mine hooked up to my iPad and my laptop simultaneously and the switching is seamless. Overall, for $129, getting these headphones plus the bonus hardshell case is a steal. Highly recommended!

For the price point, you really can't beat these. Especially since you can hook them up to computer, consoles, phones etc. Discord certified as well. text: I use these for call of duty and youtube some times my oculus 2. The rgb works with a smooth fade out loop although it's not addressable rgb it's pretty nice they even send adapters so it you want to power the rgb when your on xbox or ps5 it's independent from the controller power source (batteries) for pc you can just hook it up. Con the volume and mute switch on mine usually faces inward not a huge problem but sometimes moving around with raise or lower the volume a minor inconvenience if I would have designed it one would be on each side instead. Pros decent sound quality yesterday it saved me from being shot from behind in cod mw 2.0 the surround sound is decently crisp. The company is eager to please to to make sure you like it by the time I got it and started using it after a week they were checking up on how I liked it. After a couple messages back and forth he/she told me about the rgb being independent from the jack. Most of the rgb wired headphones I've seen aren't separated and only work on the pc. Now even watching TV (I have a projector) I can use the rgb. Microphone works as it should noise canceling I never hear complains from other players. The top fabric for comfort is comfortable I'm keeping the plastic they package it with to keep it clean. Each ear side get an addition inch approximately of length for people with bigger heads. The plastic ear cuffs are not going to flex unless to beat it with a heavy object the head band has just enough flex to it. And the microphone can be moved as it's flexible but also turned all the way pointing up . The head jack and usb splitter is pretty long about 6ft or so and it comes with a quality velcro cord piece to roll up for non use meaning if you wanted to you could put it on a headphone stand and hide the cords from you cat so it's not dangling. Suggestions to the company slightly thicker ear cups would be nice I got big ears so they kinda touch the inner fabric of the cuff and if I squeeze a little bit there's a little but of suction in my ears. Change the volume scrolling wheel to a sliding one and put it opposite of the microphone mute switch. Possible issues sometimes the microphone doesn't register on cod mw 2.0. However this is more likely to be call of duty chat program than the headphones because it happened multiple times with other headphones as well.

My son says he almost never has to charge it due to the long battery life. It was well worth it. Had some connection issues, use the phone app instead to update and later deleted it from my phone which fixed the problem. Sound is amazing and so is the battery life. For this price point I was not expecting it to be a top competitor since it is cheaper then most so a lot of value for money. Very light weight, comfortable, and functions at peak. Would recommend. These headphones are a great price. The sound quality (when they work) is really good for the price. They are very lightweight and not uncomfortable. Now for the bad; They don't work, or rather they stop working and need to be rebooted/connected or require a computer restart daily. I wear a headset a LOT, 3+ hours per day at a minimum. I have had these for 3-4 days and am returning them. I primarily used these headphones with my desktop PC using the 2.4 ghz wireless connection. Just before writing this review I sat down and put these on my head and for the third day in a row they just don't work. I have to open volume mixer and make sure they are still connected, then turn the headphones off and unplug the dongle. Today I did all of that stuff and they still don't work. The volume knob is adjusted to max volume and the PC sound works fine out of the speakers if I swap to them. They are in wireless mode and the dongle shows a "green" connected light. On paper everything is working, but no sound. I worked for a decade in PC shops building custom PC's, helpdesk, virus removals, upgrades, etc. if you have to be more knowledgeable than me to get these things to work the problem isn't the consumer.

text: Honest review, So I will start off by saying I've had a apple watch series 5 since it came out. Never had a issue with the watch other than battery life wasnt that great. I just got the s26 ultra and watch has just become a digital clock. I needed something that worked with my new phone. After researching tons of watches I came across this gem. I was happy with the price and it had all the features I needed. From what I learned you can't tap to pay with it but I never even used that on my Apple watch but for notifications, tracking my fitness activity and contolling my music. This watch is perfect. You can answer calls, reply to text and stay connected. I can't always have my phone in my hand at work so seeing my notifications are important to me. I got the watch with 56 percent battery life 3 days ago and I'm at 39 percent now. This is the first watch light enough and comfortable enough to even sleep with so I am now also tracking my sleep patterns. I haven't written a review on Amazon in ages but I honestly must say Amazon did a amazing job with this watch. It has so many face watch options and very easy to connect to the phone. If your looking for a fitness tracking good quality watch for a affordable price. I truly highly recommend this watch. I am very happy with my purchase and even if I decide to go back to the apple iPhone. I will still choose to stay with this watch over paying a few hundred dollars for a watch that I have to charge daily and don't use half the features it has. The one thing I'm not sure if this has that I know can come in handy is the sos feature but for 80 bucks I will not complain. Update. I received the watch on March 20 and lost the charger puck right away. Went on a vacation. Watch did not die till April 1st. Ordered a new charger puck that arrives today. I changed all the settings to make it last while out the country but for the watch to last almost 14 days on half battery I will say is amazing. Overall, this smartwatch delivers everything I need and more with smooth performance and a bright display, all at a very competitive price. Highly recommended for anyone wanting a feature-rich, easy-to-use smartwatch that feels premium without the premium price.
