text: I've had this watch about 2 weeks and I love it. It's very comfortable to wear. It looks great and it does what I need it to do. I didn't need a fancy smart watch. I was really looking for a fitness tracker. Before this watch I had a Garmin Vivosmart 4 but after 5 years the band broke. I didn't like that the Vivosmart 4 had a very sold part that was built into the silcone band. Once it broke I was unable to repair the band. I like that this watch has a band that can be replaced and I'll probably get a few replacement bands just to be sure this lasts a while. I don't change my tech out very often. I'm using the band with a Samsung Galaxy FE23 phone so it syncs up easily. The accuracy seems decent but I wasn't expecting it to be perfect anyway. I haven't tried out the GPS connection and tracking yet. I do a lot of parade marching in the summer and it'll be getting a good test then. The watch is very easy to charge and charges quickly. The one feature that I kind of wish it had would be contactless payment but that's not a deal breaker for me. One thing I was concerned about with this watch was that it wasn't coming directly from Samsung and it's an international version that doesn't have a US warranty. I bought the Asurion warranty just to be safe. What I got was NOT a used product. It was new and in a sealed box. I took photos as proof. When the watch started up the text was in Spanish but the first thing to do was setting up the language so that was easy to fix. I definitely recommend this watch if you're looking for just a fitness tracker. It's a very good product so far. It's at an excellent price point compared to other smart watches. Also the seller seems reliable. I have no complaints with what I received. This is my first smart watch after a decade with various Fitbits. I wanted a basic health tracker with a few extra bells and whistles, but I did not want one to answer calls and text messages. PROS: The Fit3 synced easily with my Samsung S20. The face and tile options are simple to set with the Wearable app and offer lots of flexibility. I like having my calendar available, as well as local weather. I think the battery will be decent too. In two days

text: my Samsung S20. The face and tile options are simple to set with the Wearable app and offer lots of flexibility. I like having my calendar available, as well as local weather. Wish it came with a smaller band and was easier to get on and off. I have a small wrist and there's a lot of extra band to conceal. I also hope the band loosens up eventually. There is a learning curve with the Samsung Health app and tbh, I might miss the Fitbit app with its exercise history feature. Overall, I am very happy with this device. At $50 it is half the cost of a Charge6 and has some great features. UPDATE: I've had the watch for 3 weeks and am very satisfied with its performance. Once I found the Samsung Health app (which is actually a widget) and watched a YouTube video, I am more comfortable with the app. Had an issue with the weather app sync, but restarting my phone resolved the problem. Battery is very good; however, with the Samsung Health sleep tracker I can only get 3 days on a charge. Without the sleep tracker I can get 6 days. I am not a fan of wearing the watch at night, but the sleep data I got the first week was enlightening and useful. I also found a smaller after-market band with a cutout pattern making it more breathable. My last Fitbit lasted over 3 years so I'm praying this one has some staying power too!! My wife and I have been fitbit fans since the Charge 2 (2016). Before the google purchase, fitbit was a strong leader in lower cost fitness trackers. It did concern us that our fitbits would die frequently (nearly each one we had died within a year from the Charge 2, 3, 4). However after talking with customer service they would send us replacement. So, ok, we can deal with that. (Even though in the past few years you had to provide a ton of documentation including, IIRC, your drivers license.) I had a fitbit
product_name: SAMSUNG Galaxy FIT 3 [2024] 1.6" AMOLED Display | 14 Days Battery Life | 100+ Watchfaces | 100+ Exercise Modes | International Model - (Gray)

text: with customer service they would send us replacement. So, ok, we can deal with that. (Even though in the past few years you had to provide a ton of documentation including, IIRC, your drivers license.) I had a fitbit 6 that I got for Christmas 2024. Wow, a color screen. LOL. I needed a replacement as the screen on my Charge 5 kept getting dimmer, and dimmer, to the point when I could not read it outdoors and barely indoors. So I was gifted the Charge 6 and it was big step up. But it took me a few months until I realized that the floors option was gone, not just not working, but removed, to make space for other features I could care less about. So I looked around in late June and discovered the Fit 3. So I bought one. I am SO GLAD I did. What do I need? Step counter, heart rate, floors, distance, calories. Oh, and the kicker for me was AIRPLANE MODE. I study various energetic sciences including BioGeometry and the EMF coming from our devices is not benign. I don't wear a tracker to sleep, don't want anything live, and keep my blue tooth turned off unless I am actively syncing. This is what I see comparing Fit 3 to Fitbit Charge 6. What the Fit 3 has as an advantage: Larger screen, airplane mode, beautiful faces, much cleaner UI on the watch and in the android app(s) (I am using this with a Pixel 7 phone). The ability to sync only turning on bluetooth. A few years ago fitbit/google basically said we aren't syncing your fitbit unless you have both location/GPS and bluetooth turned on. Sometimes you could get the fitbit to sync with location off but this was tricky/spotty. What advantages did the Charge 6 have for me, personally. None really. It cost about 3x the amount for the Charge 6 compared to the Fit 3. That's ridiculous. In a head to head comparsion of measurements this is what I see. Fitbit Charge is often slower to start your step counts and the Fit 3 is quicker on that. So you steps counts are going to go up and perhaps the Fit 3 does count some arm motions as steps when it should not. But the Fit 3 is still very close (within 1-3%) with the Charge
product_name: SAMSUNG Galaxy FIT 3 [2024] 1.6" AMOLED Display | 14 Days Battery Life | 100+ Watchfaces | 100+ Exercise Modes | International Model - (Gray)

text: on that. So you steps counts are going to go up and perhaps the Fit 3 does count some arm motions as steps when it should not. But the Fit 3 is still very close (within 1-3%) with the Charge 6. The Fit 3 tends to undercount calories compared to the Charge 6, I can't tell which is more accurate. On the Charge 6 bluetooth syncing was always a laborious pain taking sometimes minutes to get the past few days of data and often having to turn bluetooth off/on and/or restarts to get it to sync. The Fit 3 is very fast *most* of the time. I did have one instance where the Fit 3 was not syncing in the Health App and I had to open the Wearable app and trigger a sync from there, which worked. The big thing is battery life. I have automatic activity tracking turned on and except when syncing in the evening I keep it on airplane mode. I charged it overnight last Thursday/Friday before I first put it on. It's now Wednesday evening, nearing 6 complete days of use and my battery level is at 55%. That frugal energy use is really good and is on par with the earlier monochrome fitbits. I am very pleased with my Fit 3 purchase. Back in the early to mid 2000's I was very excited about Timex was doing just in digital watches (Iron Man, etc). The Fit 3 harkens back to those kind of days for me when you could get a very full functionality wrist device that looked good, worked great, and was a good deal. This is definitely worth a close look if you don't need an higher priced fitness/communication device that is emitting EMFs into your pericardium meridian 24x7. Well done.
product_name: SAMSUNG Galaxy FIT 3 [2024] 1.6" AMOLED Display | 14 Days Battery Life | 100+ Watchfaces | 100+ Exercise Modes | International Model - (Gray)

text: BATTERY LIFE I waited until I had to charge the keyboard before writing up this review. I received this the first week of December, charged it, and now this is 3/20/24 (almost 4 months later) and I am now charging it for the first time. I actually forgot that this was a rechargeable. I was looking for the battery slots and then remembered I had to plug it in. The keyboard light (red) lightning bolt had come on a few times but I figured I would just wait until the battery went dead. Then just a moment ago, I couldn't type any longer. As soon as I plugged it in where it's getting a charge, I was able to start using it immediately. I like that I can also turn it off when I go out of town so that it preserves the battery, although, admittedly I sometimes forget about this features. I use this keyboard daily as I do trading in the morning and I also do consulting work. I'm a techy person and just enjoy being on the computer. ERGONOMICS I personally love the feel of this type of keyboard with shallow keys that I can feel being pressed. I used to have a bluetooth HP keyboard that felt like this and went out, causing me to buy this one. It has a full keyboard and number pad. I use the Fn lock so that the volume, play controls work since I don't use the regular F keys as often. CONNECTION I had no issues connecting this to my computer. I plugged in the usb dongle, turned it on, and my laptop found it immediately. VALUE As of today, 3/20/24, this keyboard is only around $25 and I think this is a great value. CON The only con is that it isn't spillproof. My last keyboard, I accidentally dripped coffee onto it and it quit working. I believe this one will be the same, so I am trying to be very careful. Mine shipped directly from Arktek and arrived right away. It's been a great keyboard and I truly recommend it. GET THIS. I needed something quick because my keyboard failed in the middle of a workday and I didn't have time to look too deep. This had 3-hr delivery for me (delivered in 2). I DON'T REGRET MY CHOICE. text: I ve been using this ergonomic wireless keyboard and mouse for a bit now, and I m honestly really happy with it. It s super lightweight, and setup was as simple as plugging in the USB receiver; everything worked right away without me having to mess with any settings. The keyboard feels great to type on. The keys are soft and quiet, and the adjustable tilt makes a noticeable difference for my wrists during longer work stretches. I also like having the full number pad and the shortcut keys, little things, but they make my day-to-day work smoother. The mouse is comfortable and easy to control, and I haven t had any issues with the wireless connection at all. No lag, no random disconnects. Battery life has also been impressive so far. Overall, it s easy to use, comfortable, and does exactly what I need it to. I m definitely happy with the purchase. The wireless keyboard & mouse is being used with an aging laptop - the laptop's keyboard & touchpad were almost unusable, but the laptop itself (with a few minor upgrades) performed well considering its age. The keyboard uses one (1) AAA battery, is full size, and operates smoothly with no 'hiccups' or annoyances; the mouse uses one (1) AA battery, has auto-sleep built-in that kick in after approx 5 minutes of non-use, that takes a bit of getting use to bc you have to left or right click to wake it before it will respond to any motion - that's not really a 'con' bc that function could potentially extend battery life. It's an inexpensive way to get a new & functional input interface for your computer.

text: I'm in IT - I'm at a computer all day, every day. I had a boss early in my career who had severe carpal tunnel issues and he always preached ergonomics. Due to that I've been using an ergonomic keyboard for the last 17 years, specifically the Microsoft Ergonomic 4000. That keyboard is no longer produced but I've loved it, it's held up well, and I've never had any issues with my wrists from typing all day. I recently got a new job and decided to upgrade my keyboard for the first time since 2007. The Logitech ERGO K860 came up in most searches for the best ergo keyboard in 2024 so I decided to give it a shot. I couldn't be happier with it. The shape and key placement is very similar to my beloved MSE 4000. They keys are much quieter and very pleasant to type on. I was a little concerned that the "scooped keys" in the product description would be deep divots like I've seen on some keyboards and don't care for, but I needn't have worried. These are just ever-so-slightly scooped, in a way that's barely noticeable until you focus on it, but enough that it does make a positive impact on typing. It's well constructed and feels like it'll hold up for a long time (we'll see if I can get another 17 years!). The wrist rest is really comfy, nice and wide, and not a material that seems like it will wear out or get gross over time. It connected easily to two laptops and my phone, one laptop with the USB receiver and the other two via Bluetooth. Typing is nice and responsive and I don't detect any delay with typing. I like that the function keys can be customized via the Logi Options+ app, although the default functions are pretty good right out of the box. I already had the Logi Options+ app for my MX Master 3 mouse (best mouse I've ever used!) but it wouldn't recognize the keyboard until I updated it to the latest version, not a big deal though. The only change I'd make to it is the batteries. It runs on two AAA batteries. That's fine I suppose, I do have rechargable AAAs I can use. I'd prefer that the keyboard be USB rechargeable like the MX Master 3 mouse - charge it once every few months and then don't
product_name: Logitech Ergo K860 Wireless Ergonomic Keyboard - Split Keyboard, Wrist Rest, Natural Typing, Stain-Resistant Fabric, Bluetooth and USB Connectivity, Compatible with Windows/Mac, Black

text: It's taking a little getting used to the slightly different arrangement of some keys but man... overall, this keyboard KICKS TAIL!!! I love how it lights up, I'm always working in low light or the dark and my eyes aren't what they used to be so I just can't tell you how much of a difference it makes. I also feel like the key symbols won't/can't wear out because they're backlit. Typical keyboards have letter and words wear off over time, but I don't see how that's possible with this keyboard. It's not a "gamer keyboard" it's just a great basic keyboard with all the functions you need. Keystrokes feel amazing! Not sure how to describe but when you're typing it just feels really nice. A real quality built product and worth every penny. I'm considering buying another before they don't make them anymore... LOL Oh, the battery lasts forever on this thing too. You can turn it OFF when you're done for the day and you can set the keyboard to pair/work with multiple computers (even have keys configured differently if you like). The backlight shuts off when you remove your hands from the keyboard or stop typing for about 5 seconds, but its got this really cool "motion detection" feature that once you put your hands over the keys again or start typing it lights right back up! I'm telling you if you're in the market for a new BLUETOOTH keyboard and are willing to pay a little more for a NICE one... this is the board to have!!! I really love it. I'll give you the 1 thing I didn't like about it as I began using it... The FUNCTION ROW of keys has no space above the NUMBERS or Number pad, etc. So it's super easy to hit the wrong keys sometimes. I'll often go to use the "home" key and end up hitting the key to switch between machines, or the biggest one is the "lock" key the farthest top right key over the number pad's "minus/dash" key. I often go to hit the "minus/dash" key and end up locking my windows (having to sign in again). HOWEVER, using the Logitech software that is used to configure the keyboard I was able to just disable that lock key. I don't need a KEY for that anyway. When I want to lock my Windows session (log out) without shutting down, I'll

The tactile switches feel amazing - quiet enough for video calls but still give you that satisfying mechanical feel. Way better than the mushy membrane keyboards I used to deal with. The key travel and feedback is spot on. Battery life is incredible - I m getting weeks of heavy use before needing to charge, and it charges fast via USB-C. The backlight is a nice touch for late night work sessions and looks clean without being distracting. Build quality is solid Logitech - feels premium and sturdy, not like some of the cheap mechanical boards out there. The wireless connection is rock solid, never had any lag or connectivity issues. Only took me a day or two to get used to not having dedicated arrow keys and function row, but honestly I don t miss them. The function layer works great and keeps everything compact. If you re looking for a high-quality compact mechanical keyboard that doesn t compromise on feel or reliability, this is it. Worth the investment.

text: I LOVE this keyboard!! After spending two weeks researching I decided on the Aula F99 and I am so happy with my decision. I m not a gamer but wanted a full keyboard for work and this checked all the boxes. The sound is so creamy and reminds me of marbles or smooth stones. It s so fun to type on and the RGB is kinda fun too. Bluetooth connected with ease, so did wired. Haven t tried the dongle yet but so far it s been plug and play play with no issues. Feels sturdy not cheap. Sounds great out of the box. Ordered the Gatreon Milky Yellow switches but I may not even need them cuz I m kinda digging this sound already. It s soooo satisfying. Great value!! I just received my shiny new mechanical keyboard today. I've been typing on it for some hours now and I must say that this keyboard is the BOMB! My fingers seem to glide across it while typing! The key caps are so smooth to the touch. I love that I can actually type faster without making as many typos as I would on a regular style keyboard. This keyboard is a game changer for anyone who needs to do heavy duty typing tasks. I must also add that it has the most addictive creamy clicking sound! And the RGB lights are really cool and fun to see. Another plus is the tri-mode connection feature. The USB receiver has a slot that stores it right on the keyboard itself. I really am pleased with the more compact size of this full keyboard to save room on top of the desk. It's substantial in its weight, not flimsy at all! I realize that I should have been buying a mechanical keyboard years ago because the other popular keyboards are not better than this one. Take note that you can change the keycaps anytime you want to change the look and style of your keyboard. You can only do this with a mechanical keyboard. The keycaps are not expensive either as I have already done research here on Amazon. I plan to purchase a couple of keycap sets in the future. Overall, I highly recommend this particular keyboard from AULA, I believe the price point is good for the quality of the keyboard. I would only recommend that they include the keycap tongs and a few gaskets with this keyboard as other manufacturers do. Bought this for my wife s WFH setup. She fell in love with the Creamy Switch craze on TikTok and this was a reasonably priced keyboard

Sometimes it connects and sometimes it does nothing and I have to just kind of wait as there is no obvious feedback of connection or not. I think blinking blue light is attempting to connect and solid blue light is connected? but that's not always accurate on my device. Never auto connects to my iOS devices - I have to go to the settings and pair, every time. This could be an iOS problem though, I never have consistent luck with bluetooth devices and iOS. - Biggest faulty issue is my power switch doesn't work. I thought it was working fine until I realized one day when I faced a stuck key problem that when I looked at the powerkey, it was off, yet I was connected to a device. I then pressed the connect button and it tried to connect - while off. So basically, I can't turn my device off, which may lead to the next issue I have. Also, toggling the power button triggers a reconnect - so the power button does SOMETHING, but I have no idea what. - Batteries are dead after about 4 weeks of very intermittent use. I'm only in my home office for 1 day a week, so this keyboard got a maximum of 8 days worth of typing over the course of a month (being very generous with the amount of usage, it's probably closer to 5 days of use). Four weeks is a terrible battery life but maybe mine is defective. I use other logitech products which last much longer under much heavier use. Pros: - Solid construction. - Keyboard types nicely. text: Really happy with this Redragon wireless keyboard and mouse combo. It was super easy to set up just plug it in and it works right away. The keyboard feels nice when typing and gaming, not too loud but still has a good click feel. The lights look really cool, especially at night. The mouse is comfortable and moves smoothly, and I like that you can change the DPI. No lag or connection issues so far, which was something I was worried about with wireless. Battery life has been pretty solid too. Overall, great quality for the price. Definitely worth it if you want a clean wireless gaming keyboard and mouse I'd never had wireless keyboards or mice until recently. Wanting to get away from "wire spaghetti" on my desk made getting a set a logical choice. The first one I bought was from SABLUTE, yeah, I hadn't heard of it either but it sounded good in the reviews. Within the first day I noticed that the keys were sticking. Within the first week I noticed that the paint on the clear keys for the backlighting was wearing through. I returned it to Amazon. THEN I bought this set. It was a little more pricey but at least it was a brand I could find a real webstore for! Once I received it I charged them completely before trying them out. There was no more sticking keys & so far there's no sign of wear on the keys either. It is a full size keyboard. The mouse is full size as well. (I hate the mini-mice!) The buttons on both the mouse & KB are GREAT. I'm still getting used to adjusting my mouse speed & using the side buttons for going back & forth on my browser. Functionally, I can't complain at all! The build quality is quite obviously superior to the other brand. I have to charge my mouse more than the I do the keyboard but the batteries on both seem pretty good, for the moment. The only downside is that if you use both mouse & KB controls be careful about the keyboard falling asleep. It can take five to ten seconds to reconnect so make sure you toggle keys frequently to keep it awake for if you're going to be gaming with these. The key takeaway is that this KB is of higher quality than the competitor brands here on Amazon, at least compared to the other I tried & is worth the ten or so extra bucks.
product_name: Redragon S101M-KS Gaming Keyboard and Mouse Wireless with Tri-Mode, RGB Keyboard and 4800 DPI Gaming Mouse, 10 Independent Multimedia Keys for Wins, PC, Computer, Wireless S101 Ideal for Gamer

text: Easy to set up. I love that it comes with spare switches, and it sounds SOO nice! Very creamy. Excellent price point and perfect size for my desk. I have a large desk but I use a lot of the space and it was a pain to always move my bigger full-size keyboard out of the way. This is perfect. The *only* thing I wish it had were the home and end keys (because I use them with some of my programs,) but it's nothing I haven't been able to work around. The colors are soft, I love that the lighting shines through the keycaps and the lighting choices are not only easy to change, but offer a decent variety. It's got a bit of weight to it compared to some other keyboards I have used, but it's not terribly heavy, and honestly, it doesn't feel cheap. I really like the volume dial in the corner too. That's a feature i never had before and I quite like it. All in all, if you are staring out with smaller keyboards, or looking to replace one you had, this is a solid choice for someone on a budget and it doesn't feel like you are skimping on quality either. I love it. Needed it to game late at night without keeping everyone up in this tiny apartment. Super simple to set up. Literally just plug-and-play. I'm using the 2.4ghz dongle, not bluetooth so I can't speak on that. It's quick and responsive. Battery is long lasting. I forget to turn it off every night but thankfully it puts itself in sleep mode quickly. Easy to clean. The sound is quiet. Not sure what to compare it to, but much quieter than my usual keyboard. It's not 100% silent, if you're typing up an essay or something, you are going to hear keys clacking. But it's going to be much quieter than other boards. Still feels like a good mechanical keyboard. I thought maybe the keys would feel a bit sluggish or sticky, but no, I actually barely notice a difference in terms of feel. I think it's at a great price point. I got it for like $40-50 I think? Definitely would have paid $80-100 for this. You get USB-C wired, Bluetooth (3.0/5.0), and 2.4 GHz wireless modes. Super flexible for switching between different devices. Its incredibly easy to setup
product_name: Redragon K673 PRO 75% Wireless Gasket RGB Gaming Keyboard, 3-Modes 81 Keys Compact Mechanical Keyboard Hot-Swap Socket, Dedicated Knob Control and 5-Layer Noise Dampening, Linear Red Switch

text: ? Definitely would have paid $80-100 for this. You get USB-C wired, Bluetooth (3.0/5.0), and 2.4 GHz wireless modes. Super flexible for switching between different devices. The volume control knob was a must have for me, and it feels very high quality, not cheap plastic like a lot of other keyboards on the market. Typing feels refined and stable, the keyboard has a nice sound it it, definitely not silent but not loud. Build quality feels good, definitely better than what you would expect at this price. The layout keeps things compact but still includes the top row of function keys, so you re not missing out on key functionality. Great battery life, I only charge it once every few weeks with heavy use. Fully RGB backlighting with a lot of effects, plus you can tweak things in Redragon s software. The lights are very bright and have very smooth effects, its even compatible in SignalRGB! If you want a versatile, compact, and customizable keyboard that works wired or wirelessly, the K673 PRO is a really solid pick.

text: The keyboard itself is amazing. Build quality is excellent, the keys themselves are buttery smooth and have a pleasing sound. Mine is attached via USB-C but having the option of Bluetooth and wireless are nice to have. Coming from a full-sized keyboard I was initially worried about going to a compact "96%" layout, but I completely adapted to the smaller layout in ten minutes. The software, however is a real sticking point. I thought other reviews were exaggerating about the awfulness of the Armoury Crate software, but if anything they undersold it. You have to register to access full functionality and even then, the Aura Sync feature is utterly broken, which means you're limited to basic, one layer RGB effects. After around 4 hours of troubleshooting I gave up and switched to third-party RGB software and it works great. At the sale price, this keyboard is a great buy as long as you understand the software to control its lighting does not work. Nice and buttery with the Snow Linear switches, this is the best "clunk" I have had on a keyboard. Its is slightly heavy, but that just adds to the premium feel of the keyboard. Typing is great, I love how the keys are sloped and gaming is even better, so responsive, almost too responsive at times, but that's a fair trade for me. The Only bad thing is the Armoury crate software and its extra "gear" add on. Its not the worst software but it is an integrated nightmare at times. For the price, this is a decent value, there may be more value out there, but I have this way up in A tier. Battery lasts a very long time while gaming and every day use. I wanted a keyboard that had BT, RF and wired connectivity, and backlight with long battery life. on paper this keyboard met that criteria. I had a Logitech G515 and was not impressed on first opening. it had a laughable battery life, and you cannot change anything (lights, etc) without logitechs software, and you can't read the keys without the light on! Coming from the logitech, when i opened the package of the Asus ROG Strix Scope II keyboard, my only words were WOW! Packaging was excellent and top notch, the keyboard was firm, keys sounded great and the lighting is amazing! It is also a fully size keyboard (keys) in a 96% footprint! The sound of the keys are muffled but very

text: WOW! Packaging was excellent and top notch, the keyboard was firm, keys sounded great and the lighting is amazing! It is also a fully size keyboard (keys) in a 96% footprint! (Even with the LEDs off! lol) You can easily change the colors and functions on this keyboard without the software which is exactly what i was looking for. if you use the software you can do even more! All around this keyboard is fantastic, and it puts the logitech keyboard to shame at about the same price. Too soon to tell on the battery life but on paper it is 1500 hours. (the logitech is 36! lmao) I am very happy I got this keyboard and highly recommend this keyboard if you are considering a wireless option for games or office!

text: Functionality Great to use if you have a school provided laptop with tiny buttons especially if you re a teacher. Battery life If you leave it turned on it does well with being left on. It just works Keyboard The keyboard is super easy to use. It s a key board and it works well. Size Standard typing keyboard size. Not too big and not too small just right. I updated my review to let you know that this keyboard and mouse are great! I think I had too much signal interference, which is probably why the connection wasn t stable. It s been 2 years using this on the daily, and it still works perfectly! A few of the letters are worn off, but I don t watch myself type, so that s fine. I DEFINITELY recommend this keyboard and mouse! I actually have two sets, one at work and one at home. Old review: When this is working, it s excellent! Unfortunately, the keyboard stops responding while typing, but I know that it registers because the arrow keys still work. The mouse is very glitchy and jumps around. I ve changed batteries, restarted my computer, plugged the adapter into a different port, disconnected and reinstalled the device, and the results are the same. I get about 30 minutes of work (sometimes fewer) before it acts up. I want to return it, but I m 6 days past the window. Go figure! As someone obsessed with all things rose gold, this set fits right into my aesthetic perfectly, adding that touch of elegance I've been looking for. Right out of the box, the setup was super easy. The design of both the keyboard and mouse is stunning. They're incredibly sleek and ultra-slim, which not only looks great on my desk but also makes them easy

text: Small, light weight, easy to connect & very responsive. I have 2, one in black & purchased a 2nd one in silver. Both work perfectly & I highly recommend. When I first pulled this out of the box I wanted to say "Cheesy" because it is light. But it stays put on my desk. I bought this specifically because it runs on batteries. For several years I have used old Apple pint size Magic Keyboards because they are battery powered. But the key mapping for Windows is a hassle. I have spent $$ on iClever, Microsoft, and no name Bluetooth rechargeable devices and none of them has held a charge worth a *&^#. Worse, as the power drains, they develop odd behaviors. They might as well be tethered. I know it's old school, but wireless plugs in and works, batteries last until they don't but usually in the six month or least long enough that I've forgotten when I replaced them. No glitches, no dropped BT, no conflicts and this keyboard is the perfect size. I'm sure accountants and others might like a full size numeric keypad version but for what I do and the space restraints this unit is perfect. It's not in a fancy aluminum case but it didn;t cost a fortune, either. And it works. Works ok, but not very sturdy. It fits nicely in the travel nag for my laptop. One of the keys has come off. text: This keyboard works well. It is an average full size keyboard with number pad. It connected easily to the computer with the included dongle. There is a rounded dip in the keys, which is a little more than usual, more than I am used to, but okay if you like that feel of typing. I like the feature of the on-keyboard light display for capslock, numlock, scroll lock on/off and not depending on an on-screen notification which does not show on some computers/monitors. It is described as having quiet keys, however, I don't think they are all that quiet... not bad, just my opinion. Overall, the touch feels comfortable, and this is a good keyboard. I do not usually write reviews but I'm so pleased with this keyboard! I hesitated ordering it for a couple of reasons....first of all, it seems really cheap and I was afraid it would be a piece of junk...secondly, no one could assure me that it would work with my Apple imac....but I made the choice to try it thinking I'd most likely have to return it...it worked beautifully...I plugged in the toggle on the back of my computer and inserted the battery in the back of the keyboard and it popped up "can't identify the keyboard"....I opened systems and clicked keyboard and it instantly located it and that was it!!!! Up and running like a pro....I love the feel of the keys...the small indention on the keys is perfect and it is very quiet. Not one complaint with this keyboard. My old one was black and I like the white much better also. Buy this economical keyboard and you won't be sorry. I love it.....and Yes, it works with Apple imac!

text: I'm puzzled when I read the negative reviews, about this camera not working with [x]; this is my 3rd Logitech camera, and I've never had a problem getting them to work when I've reviewed the system requirements and made sure I was on a compatible machine running appropriate software. I've split use of this camera between my Mac Mini and my Macbook Air, and had no problem getting it to work on either machine, nor have I had any issue switching back and forth between them. Picture and sound quality are excellent, and there's a free Logitech app in the App Store that lets me adjust the zoom, lighting etc that's also effective and simple to use. What's really blown me away is that it's also just as impressive now as it was almost two years ago, when I first started using it. I bought it in October 2016, to replace another Logitech camera; the cord connected to the back of the camera had frayed over time as a natural consequence of adjusting the angle over and over, making the older camera only work sporadically, and I fully expected something of that nature to happen to any camera I purchased. Not so with this one. The cord has held up, and not because I've babied it; it's just a sturdier design and tougher than the model it replaced. And while we now have Ultra HD/4K cameras and better, this 1080p is still respectable quality; it's not like the old "high def" TV a friend of mine has that's 12 years old but whose picture now looks grainy and not at all impressive compared to other newer TVs. I've used a projector as a monitor for my Mac Mini and broadcast the video from this camera onto a relatively big screen (bigger than any TV I've seen) and the picture quality is still impressive. (I hope I don't jinx myself!) I'm not accustomed to peripherals like this maintaining their function over time. Either the gadget breaks or it becomes obsolete because newer versions of it do the job so much better that it becomes a relic. Not so with this camera. I spent more than the current price (not quite double what it's going for now) and still really feel like I got my money's worth. If I needed another camera I would consider buying another one, it's that good. It's nice to not have One More Thing that needs to be replaced, that just keeps

text: I was looking for a webcam that wouldn't require me to open my laptop during meetings. The camera needed to be quality. I transported it back and forth between home and office the first week, then bought a second one for the office because I was happy with the performance and ease of use. Good picture quality and autofocus even in low lighting. Easy to Adjust. Great find! Upgraded to 5 stars after more extended use. I tested this on a Mac Mini M4 using the Apple App, Photo Booth. I had no problems with the plug and play aspects of this device. Considering the low price, I think this webcam is very good. I think the 4k video is good, and the sound is also pretty good. Both the images and the sound are quite acceptable considering the low price. There is VERY slight focus lag but it is very minor and the way web cams are used I don't see it as a problem. (See attached short sample video) The privacy lens cover is clearly an afterthought. It must be attached by the user using the included double sided tape. I suggest attaching the cover while the camera is plugged into the computer and is turned on and viewed through whatever video program is easiest for you to use. That way, you can make sure the lens opening of the cover doesn't cause any vignetting of the image. (Darkening of edges) For the user of the Mac Mini M4 in the USA, at least, I don't see much value in the control software. I think the image, sound and color control is good as is. I downloaded the control software and it can do things like change the refresh rate from 60 MHz to 50, adjust color, tint and saturation. and register the device for the warranty. But for how little this thing costs, I don't see much reason to give yet another tech vendor more of my information. I downloaded the software tried it out without registration, and uninstalled it. I'll use it if I run into any color or other set up issues. but I don't think I will leave it installed, and at the price of the camera, I doubt I will even register for the warranty. I think it offers good value for the money, and I would buy it again.
product_name: EMEET C950 4K Webcam for PC - PDAF Autofocus, USB Webcam with Microphone & Physical Privacy Cover, 1080P@60FPS, USB 2.0 Plug&Play, No Tripod Hole, Web Cam w/ 70 View for Meeting/Online Classes/Zoom

text: So much computer equipment fails on the package's promises but this one does do the job and has been working now for months. Love the quality and the resolution. We have the podcast and i use it also for Zoom meetings, crisp & clear. If you need a high-quality webcam--and who doesn't these days?--that's also good for streaming (streaming resolution for webcams is about half what the recording or photo resolution is, and not many cameras stream 1080p at 60fps), this is a great choice. I have mine mounted on an arm above my computer using the included universal tripod mount, but you can also attach it to the top of your monitor with the universal monitor mount. Image quality and detail is good enough to be a bit frightening. (Every freckle and wrinkle shows up, even in low light.) I haven't tested the microphone quality because I already use a studio mic in my office. The USB-C cable is great because that means that I could connect it to my smartphone or my traveling laptop if I wanted to, and those only have USB-C ports. And the USB-C cable is annoying because it's wired in, which means that you can't use a longer or shorter cable. Unlike a more typical webcam, you can't easily attach a privacy cover to it. But since it's an external camera, all you have to do is disconnect it. It does have a pretty obvious light on it that shows you when the camera is activated. I haven't quite wrapped my head around the Logitech Capture virtual camera software, specifically how to get it to work with my green screen. But if I use the camera directly with Zoom, the virtual green screen still works perfectly. (And Zoom still touches up my appearance so I don't see every freckle and wrinkle.) Windows did not actually recognize this as a camera until I'd installed the software, which was kind of annoying, but since then it's worked perfectly. Eventually I may manage to exploit some of the more sophisticated features, but even if I never use them, I'll still consider this camera well worth the money.

It seems they have found a solution to very dark or yellow looking image quality with this webcam. Apparently they decided to jack up the white balance for the default settings so no more dark or reddish-yellow appearance like the 1080P cams do. The down side to that is the image quality appears washed out but that can be fixed by changing the brightness and contrast settings or disabling the automatic white balance and adjusting the Gamma setting (if possible with your software). Speaking of software, this webcam does not include any, nor do they provide a link to download software, so you will have to track down something that works for you and your OS. product_name: Hprosper 2K Webcam with Microphone & Light Correction, Autofocus USB Computer Camera with Privacy Cover, Wide Angle for PC, Laptop, Mac, Zoom, Conference Room, Streaming, Gaming, Video Calls

text: I get with Linux are 640x480, 600x800, 1280x720, 1920x1080 and 2048x1536. The 2 lowest resolutions are very grainy and I can t seem much difference in the 3 highest options. 1280X720 gives you smaller viewing box while 1920x1080 and 2048x1536 tend to go full screen but no change in image quality. Pretty sad if you ask me. There is also a bit of fish bowl arc along the outer edges of the image when using it at higher resolutions as well. I wanted something to replace an old 1080p camera with wide angle that I could put in the window to watch deer and other wildlife outside and hopefully cover most of the yard. The angle is about the same but this cam does seem to have a little more coverage but only because the zoom level is farther out than my old 1080p cam that also has manual zoom, pan and tilt functions. The only thing I can do with this one is adjust the brightness, contrast, gamma, sharpness and focus with the same software I was using with the old cam that was purchased in 2014. For a new camera with alleged image quality improvements since these things came out, I expected way better image quality and more onboard functions, not less functions or worse image quality. It is nice that this cam can be mounted to a tripod for better stability and more adjust-ability since the preinstalled mount doesn t give much options in where it can be mounted. There is no (or very little) tilt adjustment so where ever you plan to place it must be fairly flat and level already. Or use a tripod. I honestly dislike the fact that the camera can t be removed from the factory base because of the limited adjust-ability which equates to very limited camera placement as well when a tripod isn t available. The tripod that came with it is also extremely flimsy and the ball pivot would just barely lock to prevent it flopping out of place. Also the footprint of the legs is quite narrow, so not very stable either. For a low cost webcam, I guess this one isn t all that bad but I still wouldn t use it for business video conferences. It is an improvement over the 1080p cam I got earlier from the same manufacturer, but not better than the one I bought in 2014.

text: This camera works great! I've always been a loyal Razer customer when it comes to their Keyboards & Mice, even recently returning a Logitech setup that Geek Squad convinced me to buy over Razer, so it was very appealing to jump on such a feature-rich camera at a great price point made by a brand I've been using for almost two decades. While I haven't tested the microphone feature, as I use a dedicated one for my streaming, the camera feature works amazingly. It has auto-zoom and auto-follow, you can customize the zoom and follow, and it comes with access to Camo Studio to really enhance the camera. I use a "fake" green screen in Camo, and my streaming software flawlessly picks it up and makes it transparent for my game overlays. I'd buy this over and over again. Beautiful quality, basically plug-and-play, easy to setup and works amazingly. The FOV is perfect for wider views, and utilizing hand tracking in VTuber software. Loaded with effects in Razer Synapse as well, on top of the Lifetime Camo software to really crank out the tweakability of settings! With a bit of adjustment, the microphone is even pretty solid! By default you can hear room echo but you can adjust it to make it sound great! Even then, didn't buy for the mic but it's a good option I'd say if you need one in a pinch for meetings, calls or gaming. It does require a good port for compatibility with 4k 30 (Gen 2 USB 3.2 at least) which is common on newer motherboards. If not, PCIE USB cards exist! The C to C is really nice too since I've not had any other use for that port on my PC. Didn't think I'd test durability but already had it fall a couple of times, as it is a bit top-heavy. But it's solid and well-built camera overall, being mostly metal (presumably aluminum). Overall, it's definitely worth the cost, and highly recommend it! Camera's quality is good, very good wide angle view, easy plug and play. Lots of adjustments in OBS software. I set it up for a camera to record Billards Matches. Works Great.

text: When I first started recording videos, I didn't have any kind of lighting. Which I quickly realized was a problem. So when I was browsing Amazon for some good lights I came across this. It had pretty good reviews and seemed reliable. After considering that I decided to buy it. I'm glad I did. Very easy set up. Simple to use and adjust. Has a decent amount of light settings. At max setting it is very bright. Sits perfectly on top of my main monitor. Cord length is rather long. Giving it a bit of versatility on where you can place it. Definitely a good price for the level of quality. I highly recommend this light. This light source is an excellent addition to any Videoconferencing setup. It is automatically detected by the Ghub app if you have it previously installed on your PC and it was instantly working on my PC within a minute of connecting the included USB cable and hanging it on my monitor. The controller software in the Ghub app is extremely easy to use and it is more convenient than manually adjusting the controls on the light when it is hanging on your monitor. The software has numerous presets for adjusting the color temperature and intensity of the light simultaneously. All-in-all I am very pleased with my purchase from Amazon and I consider the light to be a good value for the money. Ambient lights are just not satisfactory for video conferencing. So I bought the Logitech LED Streaming Light to bring my face out of the shadows (so to speak). The device serves that purpose well. It connects to a USB-A port, so is easily used on a computer, but less easily used on iPads or phones. For devices without USB-A ports, I use a power brick. The LEDs do not draw much power so won't draw down batteries by much during use. The light itself is adjustable in intensity and color. The adjustability helps it provide good, even illumination in a way that is not distracting to viewers. Further, the mount is adjustable both vertically and pivoting to angle so as to avoid strang reflections from my glasses or sharp shadowing. I give it 4 stars only because it is a competing light-source to my screen. This cannot be avoided, I think, without illuminating the entire room. Perhaps a backlight would help. Nevertheless, this is a fine product and serves my needs well. Definitely worth the price.
product_name: Logitech for Creators Litra Glow Premium LED Streaming Light with TrueSoft- Computer lighting for video conferencing, Zoom meetings, with adjustable mount and desktop app control for PC/Mac - Graphite

text: The OBSBOT Tiny 2 has completely leveled up my Twitch streams. The video quality is razor sharp with vibrant colors and smooth motion it honestly looks like I upgraded to a pro studio camera. The built-in AI tracking is awesome too; it follows me around naturally when I move without being distracting, which keeps the stream dynamic and engaging. Setup was super easy, and I love how customizable the settings are. Low-light performance is also way better than any webcam I ve used before, so I don t need to blast my room with extra lighting just to look decent on stream. For streamers, content creators, or even just video calls, the Tiny 2 is worth every penny. My audience has already commented on how much more professional my streams look. Easily one of the best gear upgrades I ve made. Can t have enough of these! Did the job well!!!! Perfect in low light as I am a dj. Quality is amazing.. a little pricy but worth it!! Easy set up, easy adjustable. From the very first webcam that I purchased back in the late 90s, I've found all of them to be disappointing to some degree. I've always felt that these cameras should have better quality. Fast forward to the 2020's - I've have tried way too many webcams in the past 5 years, ranging from $50 - $500, to use for Zoom/Teams meetings. Most of them have been mediocre or worse. In the past year, I've focused on mainly on cameras that have Windows Hello capability, which in hindsight was dumb. I thought the Dell WB7022 was an excellent camera. I couldn't have been more wrong. I'd seen some reviews that looked promising for the Tiny 2, so I decided to give it a try. (Even though it doesn't have Win Hello) OMG!!! My jaw hit the floor after seeing the first images on my machine. I am in disbelief over what this camera can do. Not only is the video quality excellent, but the PTZ (& its speed) is unreal. The ability of this camera to track my movements is absolutely amazing! The last time I've been this excited about a piece of computer hardware was around 2000 when I'd gotten a Linksys 802.11b router w/ 768k DSL link & was able to stream video to my laptop. This freakin' camera is beyond amazing! I haven't been able to stop playing around with this thing
product_name: OBSBOT Tiny 2 Webcam 4K Voice Control PTZ, AI Tracking Multi-Mode & Auto Focus, Web Camera with 1/1.5" Sensor, Gesture Control, 60 FPS, HDR Light Correction, Webcam for PC, Streaming, Meeting, etc.

text: I'd gotten a Linksys 802.11b router w/ 768k DSL link & was able to stream video to my laptop. This freakin' camera is beyond amazing! I haven't been able to stop playing around with this thing. In my search to find a great meetings camera, I've tried an AVer 520 pro2 on my home machine. The 520 is no slouch and retailed for more than $1500 a few years ago. IMO, the tiny blows it out of the water! I m going to mention this (because a lot of folks don t have common sense) - The PC that you use with this camera should be somewhat modern. Do not expect that this camera to look amazing on a Core 2 Duo processor with 2gb RAM. I believe that the OBSBOT spec page lists using an Intel 6/7th gen CPU & 8gb RAM as the minimum requirement. I don t recall this being mentioned, but a having a low-grade or better graphics card will probably be helpful as well. My system has an 11th gen Intel i7 and the cam/software utilizes 7 - 10% of CPU when running the camera at 4k/30fps. 1 - 2% of my graphics card resources are also used. (My graphics card, 3080ti, is way overkill for this application.) I d think 16gb of system RAM or higher would be more than sufficient (Again, my system is overkill at 64gb) Something to note: 60fps is only available in 1080/720. HDR will NOT work in 60fps mode, no matter what the resolution is used. However, HDR works in all 4k/1080/720 resolution settings at 30fps As with anything, you can find flaws if you look hard enough. IMO, the tiny 2 is the best camera I've ever used, period. text: This camera exceeded all my expectations crisp, clear video quality and smooth performance make it perfect for everything from streaming to video calls. Setup was super simple; I plugged it in and it just worked. The design looks sleek on my desk and it stays steady even when I m moving around a bit. Love how it allows me to focus on the content without worrying about video glitches. If you re looking for a reliable, high-quality camera that doesn t complicate things, this one s it. Probably one of the best cameras (if not the best camera) you ll get for this price. Great quality, love using it in video calls and the software it comes with even lets you tweak things a bit to your liking with things like contrast and brightness. The camera isn t too small, will fit on pretty much any computer monitor or on the table and the ring light is a nice touch. I don t know how useful it really is because it definitely gives off some light, but if you re looking for great quality in a dimly lit setting, you ll definitely want to get another light to add to the room or table top. The autofocus could use some work, as holding things up close won t really trigger the autofocus much and it takes about 20/30 seconds for it to really recognize that there s a different focal point in view, which could definitely get annoying when recording a YouTube video. I tried holding random items with labels up to the camera during a conversation with friends and it would kind of get annoying having to wait for the focus to rework itself when I m trying to show somebody something in real time and keep the conversation pushing. But nonetheless, it s a great camera and I honestly think if I got any other camera, it would be the Kiyo Pro (which hopefully comes with a better autofocus since that s the only thing this product would need any real improvement on) For the brief period time this worked, I enjoyed the camera quality and the ring light. However, it broke after less than two years of use. I used the camera infrequently- only when I wanted to use my personal desktop computer for virtual job interviews and telehealth appointments- and when it was not in use, it was stored safely in a drawer in my office with other electronic accessories. Today, mid telehealth call, the camera started to make a strange grinding noise, and then both the camera and microphone failed. text: I love this, wish I knew about them years ago. Works great, easy to use, small & compact for traveling or just for convenience. I am going to get more of these. For now I am using them to run my usb grow lights and also charge my power banks, shavers, usb kitchen gadgets, lighters, etc... Not to mention they also have a flashlight function! These are very handy to have around! UPDATE: Tested with alkaline batteries. After three hours, my 10k mAh power bank was still at 25% charge, and now the light doesn't work. Will be returning this, regretfully. There's a lot to like about this item. It's compact, with an easy-open top. The LED light is bright and steady. And the price is right. That said, it does seem to be a little slow. I tested it with the only two types of batteries I had on hand, a set of Super Heavy Duty ones from a dollar store, and a national brand rechargeable type. Alkaline batteries are strongly recommended by the manufacturer, and I don't doubt they'd yield better results than what I used. But here's what I found: After one hour, the Super Heavy Duty batteries had charged my older Android from 82% to 88%. After an additional hour, it was still there. Thinking the batteries may have died, I pulled and tested them. They were just about 35% depleted. The second test, with branded rechargeables, yielded similar results. After one hour, it charged to 95% from 88%, and wouldn't go any further. Those batteries tested out at around 30% depleted. Things I think would help yield better results: A faster cable, the proper type of battery, and charging a power bank instead of directly charging a phone. I have a few 10-20k mAh banks I'm going to fool with to see if I get better results. The bottom line? It does the job, albeit slowly, and would probably charge a small flip phone more quickly. With a good supply of AA alkalines, it's a good alternative to solar for an emergency tool kit, and worth the $7 I paid. I would buy it again.
product_name: Battery Operated Phone Charger with LED|EmergencyUSB Battery Powered Power Bank Portable 4 AA Batteries Powered Outlet (2Pack Black)

text: Jackery Explorer 2000 V2 Review: I'm referring to the equipment as portable power station / PPS / battery. PERFORMANCE: It arrived well packaged and in perfect condition, with 30% charge. Charging was very simple. There is a quiet charging mode, which takes 3x duration to full charge, but is also quieter. I thought full charge speed was a nice, quiet, pleasant white / no need for the quiet charge option. Quiet charging is unneeded for us, but a neat feature. I didn't realize when shopping that there was a light on the power station (dim, bright, SOS pattern options), so that's good. I created a Jackery account and paired the device with the app, which was all simple. I guess the best part of the app is being able to remotely see the % battery left. For what it's worth though, you can remotely turn on each segment of the power station (DC / USBs, AC / normal wall outlets, and the light) too. We unplugged the fridge from the wall and plugged it into the PPS. Treated the fridge as we normally do, opening when we needed to take something out / put something in, dispensing water. It lasted for 21.3 hours. The battery also has pass-through charging, meaning you can plug the PPS into the wall while power is on, and the fridge (or other equipment) will charge from the battery. If we're away from the house while the power comes back on, the fridge will switch to being powered from the wall (via the PPS) while the battery starts to recharge in preparation for the next power loss event. The charging speed during pass-through charging is roughly the same as charging while not powering anything (~1500W). We also tested pass-through charging by turning the breaker off (simulating power loss) while the fridge was plugged into the PPS and the PPS plugged into the wall. The transition from pass-through to battery was seamless. The fridge lights never even flickered through wall-power loss and through changing back to pass-through charging. Given this capability, I could see using it as a battery backup for our sump pump, with the sump pump always plugged into the PPS, which would always be plugged into the wall. _________(I haven't dont research to verify if there will be any effect on life of product if used that way.)__________ The alternative is the P

Dec6, 2024: Amazon is back to selling for the regular listed price of $1500, with no sale. So Jackery's Black Friday sale wasn't exclusive to Black Friday. Dec12, 2024: Amazon: sale for $900 Jackery: sale for $1100 (listed at $1500, with $400 coupon) LONGEVITY: I have only had this product for about a week, so I can not speak to the longevity of the product. text: I ve been using the Anker Zolo USB C Charger Block (2-Pack, 4-Port Wall Adapter with 50W Max Output) for a couple of weeks now, and it s become one of my favorite recent purchases for home and travel charging. Here s my honest take: Pros: Super compact and travel-friendly: These chargers are genuinely small about the size of an AirPods case and thanks to the GaN tech, they don t feel bulky even with four ports (2 USB-C + 2 USB-A). The stable prong design means they sit flush in the outlet without wobbling or falling out. Smart power distribution: The standout feature is how the primary USB-C port holds steady at a full 30W even when everything else is in use. The second USB-C gets 20W when both are plugged in, and the remaining ports share the rest (around 15W total when all four are occupied). Perfect for charging an iPhone 16/17 at fast speeds while topping up an iPad, earbuds, and maybe an older device via USB-A all from one outlet. Great value in a 2-pack: Having two of these means one stays on my nightstand for phone + watch + tablet, and the other lives in my work bag or kitchen counter. At the price point, it s a no-brainer over buying single chargers repeatedly. Reliable and safe: No overheating issues during normal use (phones/tablets), and Anker s ActiveShield tech gives peace of mind. Charges consistently without random pauses or drops (minor redistribution hiccups when plugging/unplugging are normal and brief). Wide compatibility: Works flawlessly with my iPhone, Pixel, Galaxy, iPad, and even light laptop charging if needed. Cons: Not ideal for high-power laptops simultaneously (e.g., don t expect to max out two MacBooks at once this is more for phones, tablets, and accessories). When all four ports are maxed with demanding devices, the shared power on the secondary ports drops noticeably, but that s expected at 50W total. Overall, 5 stars highly recommend if you re tired of juggling multiple single-port bricks or bulky multi-chargers. This is exactly what I needed for decluttering outlets and staying charged on the go. Anker nails it again with quality and practicality. Worth every penny! These chargers have worked great for me. text: Writing an actual review, since complaining about "false advertising" over and over doesn't really tell anyone much of anything about the product. Yes, it doesn't come with a power source, but it's still a great product. Works as well as the official Apple product, with a cord twice the length of the one Apple sells. Will be purchasing one or two more to keep around the house, because MagSafe is so much easier than having to fiddle around with a Lightning cable! Would definitely recommend this product. I put a magsafe style case on my Pixel 7 and this charger works great to charge it completely overnight. It's not a fast charger, but it does the job and I think a slower charger is better for battery life anyway. Generally, I bought this both for ease of connection and for a reduction in wear on my USB-C port, which seems to fail after a couple of years on most phones I've had. First, the six foot cord allows me to plug in farther from a power source without having to stretch the cable. Next, the soft touch material on the back of the charger helps it to not slide around when setting it on a table or nightstand. I purchased this charger for use with an iPhone14 Pro. After trying four other non Apple Magnetic chargers, this is the first one I ve found that responds with the same green circle animation response you would get from an Apple MagSafe charger. Excellent fast charger!

text: Great qualities speaker very small and the magnet is amazing. My Son put it on his E bike and it held up really great very loud for it being small and pretty clear as well. Would buy again. I saw ads on this device on Instagram that caught my attention. I watched a review on YouTube from Flossy Carter giving this device in his own words a major major major go. Amazon has this device and I went with it over the manufacturer website even though the website had it on sale for a substantial discount. I decided with Amazon simply because they had the 2 or 3 year insurance option which I didn t mind paying extra for plus I was able to get this same day versus waiting a week for delivery. I ve been playing with this device for a day and I m sold. I ve never been a MagSafe fan in general until this device. You can charge this device wired and your phone wirelessly with this magnetic device attached to it. I found that pretty neat. Sound quality has enhanced the sound of my IPhone. If I want to I can also attach this device to any magnetic surface nearby and listen in. Doubles as a phone stand too. This thing is simple to use and connect to Bluetooth. The colors it flashes adds a nice visual touch. The size of how small this thing is makes it even more bang for your buck value as you can throw this in your pocket or any bag to go. I haven t tested speaker calls yet but I am certain I will probably like that feature as well. I would recommend MagSafe compatible phone cases with this speaker. By far my favorite tech gadget of 2025 so far. The appearance of the device is also sleek. I went with the graphite color and I dig it. Lots of features and I highly recommend this product! Very nice quality speaker. The sound quality is very good for the size, not sure it gets any better at this size. Yes, it goes loud enough, bass is light as one would expect, putting it in a room corner helps a lot. It s not UE Boom levels of output, but it s not the size either. text: Excellent laptop stand very sturdy and well built. The aluminum feels solid and premium, and the stand easily holds two laptops at once without any wobbling. I also really like how easy it is to adjust the width to fit different laptop sizes. Once adjusted, it holds the laptops securely and doesn t shift. Overall, this is a high-quality, space-saving stand that does exactly what it s supposed to do. Highly recommended for anyone using multiple laptops or looking to keep their desk organized. I have 2 laptops but different sizes and WFH. I bought this because I used one before in an office job I had and really liked the convenience of it and also it gives me more space on my desk. Its pretty (I got silver because pink was a little too much $$ for me, silver was on discount) and its heavy duty. Its a brick. It's not going anywhere. This product, you are able to adjust the size of the slots for whatever you're going to use it for (laptop, tablet, etc). The tool that came with it, did not work. It was just scraping the paint off the tool. I reached out to customer service because I didn't want to return the whole product for just the tool. I apparently received an email that was in my spam and a week later, I sent another email to customer service and they politely mentioned they had reached out to me ( I confirmed it by searching my emails). They asked for my address and order #. I sent them the info and in 3 days I received a replacement. The customer service was fast and efficient and the tool works amazing and holds both laptops and I have used for about a week now and it works perfectly. I highly recommend this product if you're on the fence. I also recommend reaching out to customer service first if there is an issue. The grip on this thing is amazing. I should mention that I use it for a gaming laptop and a macbook. A huge upgrade from the laptop mount I had before. It s extremely sturdy and I love the fact you can adjust the width to hold whatever laptop or device you want! The size was smaller than I expected which is great for my small table. I really appreciated how stable it was for a small stand. Looks elegant too.

There are a couple of things to be aware of (hence the 4 and not 5-star rating): #1 In the position I have my 17 MSi laptop, it extends back past the top of the tray the computer sits on. That can cause the front of the laptop to lift up and no longer rest against the tabs that keep it from sliding down. It s workable but I m still experimenting with putting something behind the unit for support to prevent the back from pivoting down (see picture). #2 In my setup, the on/off buttons (for the fan and light) located under the front of the unit are difficult to access. I have the whole unit sitting on a speaker on my desk, so I m not looking for height from the stand - only tilt (see picture). It should be obvious; I use a separate keyboard for typing. With 45 degrees of tilt, this leaves no room to reach the buttons with my finger. I use a pencil-like tool with a 90 bend at the end to slide in and push the button. Not a deal breaker but definitely inconvenient. #3 I assumed (danger word) the fan direction would be sucking hot air OUT of the laptop, but its direction is INTO the laptop. It probably still helps with cooling but I was surprised by that

text: I've only owned this a few days now but I was pleasantly surprised at the quality and am happy with my purchase, as my complaints are small. I think the size is about 1 to 1.5 inches wider than measured, and I suspect a bit heavier than listed. There's tons of ventilation slots, the wood quality and polish seems well done, the fabric is well applied, the adjustable stand is sturdy, and the padding feels comfortable (but it's worth noting the fabric for the underside padding and wrist rest is a mildly rough texture that may bother some people). My complaints are small: the rough fabric texture, the bar to stop the laptop sliding is pretty tall for a thin laptop (but the wrist rest helps mostly negate this issue), the weight is a bit heavy, and it's a bit cramped for a 17" laptop. Some issues other users mentioned were not present in mine: the tray doesn't pop open when carrying it for me and when closed, it sits flush with the rest of the lapdesk; the fabric underneath the tray is well applied; and the logo cutout is smooth. I also am showing it size wise holding my 13" Macbook Neo, 11" iPad Air, 17" ASUS laptop, and Gen 1 LCD Steam Deck. Overall I really like this lapdesk and recommend it if you can tolerate the heavy weight and are comfortable using a large laptop on it, or have a 15" or smaller laptop. Perfection. I don't even write reviews and HAD to for this one. Superb quality. Extremely ergonomic. I have to work from various locations often due to pain/illness/ etc and it's comfortable EVERY time. They really thought of everything in this design. Plus it's aesthetically beautiful as well. If I had seen it in a store and been able to try it out, I would ve paid double for it. Worth every penny. I love this lap desk!!! I wish that I ordered it years ago!!!! So comfortable with ventilation so that the laptop does not overheat. Love the mouse pad and phone holder!!!! I would recommend splurging on this lap desk!!!

You can type on the laptop on the stand unlike some stands that wobble. Because it is made of one piece of aluminum, you cannot adjust the height. Yet, this is the perfect height for the laptop that you do not need to adjust anything. It makes your posture more ergonomic, and makes it typing easier as well. Even the angle of webcam is better on this stand. If you consider all these factors + 5 years of use and still going strong, I think this is possibly the best stand. I highly recommend that anyone contemplating this should buy one and give it try. Sturdy. Beautifully designed. Functional. This item is for when you need to raise your laptop on your desk to allow for adequate ventilation and to align your screen with an external monitor. It should be used with a wireless or wired trackpad and keyboard as it is not ergonomically designed for typing on the laptop keys. I needed a way to raise my MacBook Pro 16" on my desk so it would be properly ventilated. This item was the perfect solution. It raised the MacBook Pro laptop to a height that complemented my 29" monitor. I can still use the laptop's trackpad and keyboard while it is on the stand (but not for extended periods), but it works best with my Magic Trackpad and wireless keyboard. It's a perfect and simple design that solves a problem. I'm buying a second stand for another location.
product_name: Twelve South Curve for MacBooks and Laptops | Ergonomic desktop cooling stand for home or office (matte black)
